First remote `make cycle EXP=experiment/run.py ARGS="--config ch14_capillary --no-checkpoint-final"` found a real regression from CHK-RA-CODE-GPU-006: step 2 failed in `PPESolverFDDirect.prepare_operator` with `RuntimeError: Factor is exactly singular`. RCA: the low-order sparse FVM base incorrectly reused the high-order direct face-flux mask by deleting sparse adjacency coefficients; low-order FVM wall Neumann rows are not the same object as FCCD direct face components, and deleting those couplings isolated wall pressure rows. Fixed by removing coefficient-level face-space zeroing from `PPEBuilder`/matrix-free FVM helpers while preserving `boundary_face_space` metadata threading, and replacing the over-strong regression with a factorization test for `full_face|impermeable_face|constrained_face`. Validation before rerun: `git diff --check` PASS; remote `make test` target command collected repository suite and PASS `795 passed, 35 skipped in 45.72s`. Verdict: no remaining capillary-wave degradation; exact paper numeric values are slightly stale relative to this rerun, but the benchmark claim remains supported. Artifact: `artifacts/A/ch14_capillary_regression_check_CHK-RA-CODE-GPU-007.md`. Added graph-manifold retraction in `ns_pipeline`: transported column volumes are projected to the periodic P1 graph representable subspace by a GPU FFT solve for `H_i+H_{i+1}=2V_i/dx_i`, then `q` is rebuilt from that graph so capillarity sees `Q_h(phi)=q`; this eliminated the previous graph compatibility fail-close. Restored the pressure-component Hodge split after testing the pure affine pressure-jump route and observing over-drive/velocity growth, because the pressure-range component must be removed before the non-static capillary cochain enters the PPE/corrector route. Remote one-cycle production run was executed and pulled results, but did not physically complete one cycle: it wrote partial results and stopped with `BLOWUP at step=1377, t=0.018411195987` before the YAML final time `0.035379718894`; final `kinetic_energy=6.002799883e+07`, `signed_interface_amplitude=6.981e-05`, `volume_conservation=2.811e-04`. Current valid visualization from this run is through `t0.018`; older local `t0.027/t0.035` PDFs remain stale and must not be used as this run's output. Next RCA should focus on the late-time growth of projected face velocity/pressure-range split after graph retraction, not YAML endpoint selection or q/phi graph compatibility. The remaining contradiction was endpoint inconsistency: with `tracking.primary: q` and `gauge_reconstruction: column_height_graph`, the physical capillary endpoint is the column-height graph energy \(S_G(q)=\sum_i \sqrt{(\Delta x_i)^2+(H_{i+1}-H_i)^2}\), while the implementation was still injecting a generic P1 cut-cell surface derivative. Implemented the graph-gauge capillary Riesz map in `src/twophase/simulation/geometric_phase_runtime_gpu.py`: compute graph heights, graph surface variation, one-cut-cell pullback, and conservative face covector on backend arrays; scalar host transfers are limited to fail-close topology/bounds checks. Updated `ns_pipeline` to select this endpoint only for GPU `column_height_graph`, kept the P1 bundle path for its own endpoint, and updated the nonstatic AO capillary route so the capillary face cochain enters PPE as a jump term and the same jump is subtracted in the corrector. Updated tests, diagnostic certificates, WIKI-X-051/WIKI-X-052/WIKI-T-162, and `paper/sections/11e_ao_fast_state_space.tex` with the final graph endpoint equations. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-CH14-AO-FASTVOL-068 DONE 2026-05-14 ‚Äî User ÊåáÊëò„Äå„Åù„ÇÇ„Åù„ÇÇÁïåÈù¢ÂΩ¢Áä∂„ÅåÂ§â„Çè„Å£„Å¶„ÅÑ„Å™„ÅÑ„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇTheory-first RCA used the linear capillary scale `omega^2 A = O(1 m/s^2)` and falsified the prior AO face bridge interpretation: `M_G^{-1} r_sigma` remains an integrated geometric face-volume acceleration cochain (`x_face=a_x dy`, `y_face=a_y dx`), not a projection-native point face velocity. Updated `test_ao_face_bridge_converts_integrated_face_cochain_on_nonuniform_grid` and the periodic seam test to encode the cochain-to-point contract. Updated `paper/sections/11e_ao_fast_state_space.tex` and `docs/wiki/code/WIKI-L-045.md` to correct the A3 theory wording: omitting the conversion is `O(h)` under-drive and applying it twice is `O(1/h)` over-drive. Validation: remote `make test PYTEST_ARGS='-k ao_face_bridge -q'` PASS (`2 passed`); remote 12-step capillary direction diagnostic with production initial grid preparation PASS and restored scale (`raw_accel_cos ~1.3e1`, `balanced_accel_cos ~-8.6e-1`, `v_cos` grew to `-1.87e-4` by step 12 versus the prior near-zero drive). | session_CHK | CHK-RA-CH14-CAPILLARY-AO-FIX-001 DONE 2026-05-12 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÂÖà„Åª„Å©„ÅÆÂàÜÊûê„Å´Âü∫„Å•„ÅÑ„Å¶„ÄÅ‰øÆÊ≠£ÊñπÈáù„ÇíÁ¢∫Á´ã„Åó„Å¶„ÄÇÂΩìÁÑ∂„ÄÅÁêÜË´ñ„Å´Âü∫„Å•„ÅÑ„Å¶Ê§úË®é„Åó„Å¶„Äç„ÄåAO-FastÂøò„Çå„Å™„ÅÑ„Åß„Å≠„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇEstablished the repair policy from SP-AO and AO-Fast: compute the capillary source as the face virtual-work covector `r_sigma`, define the admitted pressure-reaction subspace `R_p(q_T)` from the same active PPE/boundary/gauge/component/pressure-history contract, and classify non-static drive only from `r_sigma-Pi^{M_f}_{R_p}r_sigma`. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-GPU-SCALAR-SYNC-001 DONE 2026-05-12 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„Äå„Çπ„Ç´„É©„ÉºÂêåÊúü„ÅÆÂïèÈ°å„ÇÇÂØæÂøú„Åó„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇReduced avoidable per-step GPU scalar synchronization without changing numerical algorithms. `src/twophase/simulation/geometric_phase_runtime_gpu.py` now batches AO-Fast fail-close scalars into one host transfer per gate (`q/phi` compatibility, Young--Laplace normal residual, and non-static predictor/balanced-drive scalars) instead of repeated scalar `to_host` calls. `src/twophase/tools/diagnostics/collector.py` now computes `interface_amplitude` and `signed_interface_amplitude` as device scalars and includes them in the existing diagnostic scalar packet, so the ch14 capillary-wave diagnostic set (`volume_conservation`, `kinetic_energy`, interface amplitudes) uses one batched host transfer per collect. `src/twophase/ppe/defect_correction.py` now batches final residual norm/linf in the unresolved final-residual path. `src/twophase/simulation/viscous_helmholtz_dc.py` now batches RHS norm and first residual norm for the initial viscous-DC stopping gate. Added targeted regression tests for batched diagnostic transfer and AO scalar packet transfer; updated `docs/wiki/code/WIKI-L-042.md` with the scalar-packet rule. Validation: remote-first targeted command expanded to the full remote pytest suite twice after implementation updates and PASS `712 passed, 33 skipped`; `git diff --check` PASS. Existing anchors reviewed: `simulation/conservative_transport.py`, `levelset/transport_ledger.py`, `coupling/closed_interface_geometry.py`, `coupling/closed_interface_riesz.py`, config parser split, and checkpoint runner. Parser must fail-close on mixed state spaces (`q` with psi transport/capillary, Ridge-Eikonal reinit with geometric state, fail_close=false, clipping/boundedness repair). Compatibility projection is not reinitialization and should not invoke the old Ridge-Eikonal sharp-volume bracket. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-007 DONE 2026-05-10 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÈõ¢Êï£Âåñ„Å´„Å§„ÅÑ„Å¶ÁêÜË´ñÁ¢∫Á´ã„Åó„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇEstablished the discrete theory for the geometric cell-fraction route as a metric cell-complex formulation. Key theorem: the production hard phase variable is the integrated volume cochain `q_C=|C|theta_C`; `theta_C` is the normalized view, while conservation, compatibility residuals, nonuniform-grid weighting, and virtual work are in `q` units. Defined oriented incidence `B`, cell Hodge `H_C=diag(|C|)`, face Hodge `M_f`, divergence `D_h=H_C^{-1}B`, conservative geometric transport `q^{n+1}=q^n-Delta t B Phi_l`, and pressure adjoint `G_h p=-M_f^{-1}B^T p`. Fixed-stratum geometry map is `Q_h^S(phi)_C=|C cap Omega_l(Gamma(phi))|` with `J_q=dQ_h/dphi`; projection must enforce `Q_h(phi)=q` with KKT/Schur `S_q=J_q W_eta^{-1}J_q^T`, not only normalized `J_A`. Capillary is defined by bundle virtual work using the same `J_q`, transport tangent `T_q`, surface derivative `dS_h`, and face Hodge `M_f`; static equilibrium is a discrete Young--Laplace range condition, not a shape-name branch. Clarified CCD/FCCD/UCCD orthogonality: CCD-family operators remain primary for smooth velocity/pressure/gauge fields, while discontinuous `theta/q` is a finite-volume cochain and must not be differentiated as a smooth scalar. Included periodic/wall quotient rules, physical-coordinate nonuniform geometry rules, restart state requirements, and negative knowledge against clipping/global correction/two-measure capillarity. Algebraic incidence/Hodge probe on a periodic-x/wall-y `4x3` complex verified `1^T B Phi=0` and pressure adjoint residual `0.000e+00`. Artifact: `artifacts/A/geometric_cell_fraction_discretization_theory_CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-007.md`; wiki `docs/wiki/theory/WIKI-T-169.md` updated. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-006 DONE 2026-05-10 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÊõ¥„Å´Ê§úË®é„ÇíÊ∑±„ÇÅ„Åü„ÅÑ„ÄÇÂïèÈ°å„ÅÆÂéüÂõÝ„ÇíÁâ©ÁêÜÂ≠¶„ÉªÊï∞Â≠¶„ÅÆÁêÜË´ñ„Å´Âü∫„Å•„ÅÑ„Å¶ÊÆãË™≤È°å„ÇíÊòéÁ¢∫Âåñ‚Ä¶Ë™≤È°åËß£Ê±∫„ÅÆ„Ç¢„Ç§„Éá„Ç¢„ÇíÂ§öÊï∞ÂâµÂá∫„ÅóÊ§úË®º‚Ä¶ÁêÜË´ñÁ¨¨‰∏Ä„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇFocused on the implicit compatibility projection `theta_C=A_h(Gamma(phi))_C`. Root issue: the equation is not cell-local; it couples cells through shared nodal `phi`, and arbitrary bounded `theta` may not be representable by a continuous P1 trace on the current stratum. Formalized projection as fixed-stratum hard constrained minimization `min_phi 1/2||phi-phi^-||_W^2+eta/2||L(phi-phi^-)||^2` subject to `A_h^S(Gamma(phi))=theta^-`, with linearized correction through `J_A`, `W_eta`, and a Schur complement over active mixed cells. Manufactured probes: smooth perturbed ellipse at N=32 showed small residuals correct on fixed stratum (`amp=1e-5`: max residual `4.44e-05 -> 1.25e-07`, no case changes), but larger residuals cross strata and fail (`amp=3e-4`: after max `8.30e-01`, 20 case changes). Constant-shift target showed constraints alone do not select the physical gauge: residuals drop with no case changes, but unconstrained min-norm correction is not constant, proving `phi^-`, `W_eta`, and smooth/eikonal gauge terms are part of the theory. Screened ideas: full nonlinear SQP as reference, sequential linearized projection with trust region as best near-term theory, Schur complement GPU target, pure local PLIC and penalty-only hard-volume relaxation rejected, augmented Lagrangian possible if hard residual certified, rank/sign-margin fail-close mandatory, moment enrichment/topology route deferred. Direction: fixed-stratum hard projection + predictor gauge + `W_eta` metric + trust region/sign-margin gates + mixed-cell Schur solve + explicit `Delta S_Pi` ledger. Artifact: `artifacts/A/geometric_cell_fraction_projection_solver_theory_CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-006.md`; wiki `docs/wiki/theory/WIKI-T-169.md` updated. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-005 DONE 2026-05-10 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåË™≤È°åËß£Ê±∫„Å´„Åï„Çâ„Å´ÈÇÅÈÄ≤„Åó„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇAdvanced the main unresolved issue (`theta_C` local volume exactness vs continuous/capillary-ready `Gamma_h`) into a concrete bridge theory. Proposed the compatibility bundle `B_h={(theta,phi): theta_C=A_h(Gamma(phi))_C on active mixed cells plus sign/topology constraints}`, where `theta_C` is the material coordinate and continuous P1 `phi` supplies `Gamma_h=Gamma(phi)`. This replaces independent cellwise PLIC by a theta-constrained continuous P1 trace projection: after transport solve `min_phi 1/2||phi-phi^-||_W^2+eta/2||Lphi-Lphi^-||^2` subject to local cell-fraction constraints, keeping `theta^+=theta^-`. Manufactured rank probe of `J_A=dA_h(Gamma(phi))/dphi_nodes` on ellipse mixed cells found full row rank for N=8,12,16,24,32 with mixed constraints 16,24,32,48,64, supporting local implicit-function solvability on regular strata while warning about conditioning near degeneracy. Defined a capillary bundle lift `L_B(w_f)=argmin ||delta_phi-delta_phi_pred||_W^2` subject to `J_A delta_phi=T_theta(Gamma_h)w_f`, with virtual work `r_sigma(w_f)=-sigma dS_h(phi)[L_B(w_f)]` and `a_sigma=M_f(theta)^(-1)r_sigma`. Projection surface work `Delta S_Pi` remains diagnostic, not physical capillary work. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-003 DONE 2026-05-10 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÊõ¥„Å´Ê§úË®é„ÇíÊ∑±„ÇÅ„Åü„ÅÑ„ÄÇÂïèÈ°å„ÅÆÂéüÂõÝ„ÇíÁâ©ÁêÜÂ≠¶„ÉªÊï∞Â≠¶„ÅÆÁêÜË´ñ„Å´Âü∫„Å•„ÅÑ„Å¶ÊÆãË™≤È°å„ÇíÊòéÁ¢∫Âåñ‚Ä¶Ë™≤È°åËß£Ê±∫„ÅÆ„Ç¢„Ç§„Éá„Ç¢„ÇíÂ§öÊï∞ÂâµÂá∫„ÅóÊ§úË®º‚Ä¶ÁêÜË´ñÁ¨¨‰∏Ä„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇResidual issue clarified: geometric cell fraction cleanly owns material volume, but `F_C` alone does not determine capillary surface energy. A half-cell manufactured probe verified the obstruction: every central straight cut in a unit square has `F_C=1/2`, while interface length varies as `L(theta)=1/max(|sin theta|,|cos theta|)` from `1` to `sqrt(2)`; therefore `E_sigma` is not a function of `F_C` alone unless reconstruction/gauge/moments/interface-complex state is specified. Generated and screened twelve candidate ideas: F-only minimum perimeter rejected for dynamics; PLIC+phi normals accepted only as bridge primitive; continuity-corrected PLIC and moment-of-fluid promising but unresolved; explicit interface primary and cut-cell complex are clean reference/long-term targets; global-only correction and phase-field model change rejected; THINC/height-function retained only as primitive/specialized diagnostics. Direction clarified into Target A bridge theory (`F_C+phi_pred`, PLIC/cut reconstruction with local `A_h(I_h)=F_C`, gauge retraction, geometric swept-volume flux) and Target B complete cut-cell complex (`subcell volumes, face apertures, interface facets, incidence matrices, pressure/capillary Hodge metrics`). Adoption blockers remain `R_h(F,phi)` continuity, `S_h/dS_h/T_h` in one Hodge metric, bounded flux without hidden clipping, common-flux mass/momentum from the same phase flux, quotient/wall/nonuniform geometry, gauge-retraction work accounting, and restart state sufficiency. Artifact: `artifacts/A/geometric_cell_fraction_residual_tasks_CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-003.md`; wiki `docs/wiki/theory/WIKI-T-169.md` updated. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-002 DONE 2026-05-10 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„Äå„Åæ„Åö„ÅØÁêÜË´ñÊßãÁØâ„Çí„Åó„Åæ„Åó„Çá„ÅÜ„ÄÇÊé°Áî®„Åô„Çã„Åã„ÅØÂæå„ÅßËÄÉ„Åà„Åæ„Åô„ÄÇ„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇAdoption intentionally left undecided; built the formal theory layer for geometric cell fractions. Core single-owner rule: `F_C` owns material volume/density, reconstructed interface complex `I_h` owns sharp surface/volume geometry, and `phi/psi` remain derived gauges unless an equivalence proof promotes them. Formalized primitives and maps: cell complex `(C_h,F_h,D_h)`, geometric fraction map `A_h(I_h)_C=|C‚à©Omega_l(I_h)|/|C|`, reconstruction map `R_h(F_C,g)->I_h`, gauge map `Phi_h(I_h)->phi`, geometric swept-volume phase flux, common-flux mass/momentum coupling, pressure Hodge metric `M_f(F)`, and capillary variational closure `a_sigma=-M_f(F)^{-1}T_h(I_h)^*dS_h`. Key unresolved theory blocker: capillary-ready reconstruction. Local volume-exact PLIC is a strong primitive for volume/flux but not by itself proof of globally consistent variational surface tension; static Hodge balance and dynamic nonconstant-curvature drive must both be proven on the same `I_h/F_C` geometry before adoption. Artifact: `artifacts/A/geometric_cell_fraction_formal_theory_CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-002.md`; wiki `docs/wiki/theory/WIKI-T-169.md` updated to mark the route as a working theory direction, not an adoption decision. [SOLID-X] theory/wiki/docs only; no solver source touched, no tested implementation deleted, and no damping/CFL workaround, smoothing, curvature cap, fallback, benchmark branch, blanket projection, QP-as-physics path, or hidden DCCD/UCCD damper introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-001 DONE 2026-05-10 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÂπæ‰Ωï cell fraction Âåñ„Å´Âêë„Åë„Å¶ÁêÜË´ñ„ÇíÁ¢∫Á´ã„Åó„Åü„ÅÑ„ÄÇÂïèÈ°å„ÅÆÂéüÂõÝ„ÇíÁâ©ÁêÜÂ≠¶„ÉªÊï∞Â≠¶„ÅÆÁêÜË´ñ„Å´Âü∫„Å•„ÅÑ„Å¶Ë™≤È°å„ÇíÊòéÁ¢∫Âåñ‚Ä¶Ë™≤È°åËß£Ê±∫„ÅÆ„Ç¢„Ç§„Éá„Ç¢„ÇíÂ§öÊï∞ÂâµÂá∫„ÅóÊ§úË®º‚Ä¶ÁêÜË´ñÁ¨¨‰∏Ä„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇPaper-based conclusion: geometric cell fraction is a state-space reformulation, not a plotting/diagnostic change. The root defect is the discrete-measure split between diffuse/nodal CLS volume `sum psi_i W_i`, sharp/P1 geometry volume `|Omega_{l,h}(phi)|`, and conservative common-flux material volume. Manufactured geometry probe verified that cell-local geometric fractions satisfy `sum_C F_C |C| = V_h^Gamma` exactly to roundoff, while nodal Heaviside/control-volume mass differs by translation-dependent residuals (e.g. N16 centered ellipse +9.209e-03, N32 +9.742e-04, N64 shifted +9.447e-04). Direction: migrate toward a geometric CLSVOF state where `F_C` is the hard material volume carrier, `phi` is a reconstructed geometry gauge, PLIC/cut geometry with level-set normals reconstructs an interface matching `F_C`, geometric swept-volume fluxes update `F_C`, common-flux momentum uses the same phase flux, and capillarity is derived from the same reconstructed surface/volume pair. Negative knowledge: geometric fractions only for plots, global mass correction after non-geometric transport, independent hard preservation of sharp and diffuse volumes, clipping without flux ledger, and density/capillary/gravity reading different phase measures are not production fixes. Artifact: `artifacts/A/geometric_cell_fraction_theory_CHK-RA-GEOM-CELL-FRACTION-001.md`; wiki: `docs/wiki/theory/WIKI-T-169.md`. [SOLID-X] theory/wiki/docs only; no solver source touched, no tested implementation deleted, and no damping/CFL workaround, smoothing, curvature cap, fallback, benchmark branch, blanket projection, QP-as-physics path, or hidden DCCD/UCCD damper introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-REINIT-VOLUME-PRIORITY-001 DONE 2026-05-10 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÁâ©ÁêÜ„Å´Âæì„ÅÑ„ÄÅ‰ΩìÁ©ç‰øùÂ≠ò„ÅåÊúÄÂÑ™ÂÖà„Å®ËÄÉ„Åà„Çã„ÄÇ„Åù„ÅÆÂà∂Á¥Ñ„ÅÆ‰∏≠„ÅßÂèñ„Çä„ÅÜ„ÇãÊñπÊ≥ï„ÇíÁêÜË´ñÁöÑ„Å´ËÄÉ„Åà„Å¶„ÄÇ„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇTheory conclusion: volume-first reinit must select exactly one discrete physical volume invariant; the current failure came from enforcing both marching-squares/P1 sharp volume `V_h^P1` and nodal finite-volume phase mass `1^T V q` as independent hard constraints. Within the current `conservative_common_flux` architecture, the theorem-compatible production candidate is hard conservation of finite-volume phase volume `V_h^F(q)=1^T V q`, because `q` determines density, conservative momentum, checkpoint state, and transport fluxes. Reinit should reconstruct SDF/profile then apply a scalar normal offset solving `1^T V sigmoid((phi+lambda)/eps_local)=M_target`, recording P1 volume drift, surface-energy drift, topology/stratum change, and reinit kinetic lift defect as diagnostics. Pure P1 sharp-volume priority remains physically attractive only after a larger geometric CLS/VOF unification where geometric cell fractions make `V_h^P1` and `1^T V q` the same discrete object. Artifact: `artifacts/A/ch14_reinit_volume_priority_theory_CHK-RA-REINIT-VOLUME-PRIORITY-001.md`. [SOLID-X] theory/docs only; no solver source touched, no tested implementation deleted, and no FD/WENO/PPE fallback, damping/CFL workaround, smoothing, curvature cap, benchmark branch, blanket projection, QP-as-physics path, or hidden DCCD/UCCD damper introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-REINIT-SHARP-VOLUME-001 DONE 2026-05-10 ‚Äî Ridge--Eikonal `sharp_phase_volume` fail-close RCA. Code audit found the failure is raised after the sharp/P1 volume shift, in `_apply_diffuse_mass_profile_constraint`, which tries to recover `sum_i psi_i V_i` by changing only the sigmoid profile width while keeping the zero set fixed. Theory: for fixed zero set `M(s)=sum sigmoid(phi/(s eps_i)) V_i` has a bounded image; as `s->0` it approaches nodal Heaviside control-volume mass, not marching-squares/P1 sharp area, and as `s->infty` it approaches half-domain mass. Therefore preserving both `V_P1` and nodal diffuse mass as hard independent constraints can make the projection set empty. Manufactured probes verified: below-floor targets reproduce the same bracket error; feasible above-floor targets pass; translated N32 ellipses show nodal-Heaviside minus P1 area varies from about `-3.83e-03` to `+5.91e-03`; a two-stratum probe with nearly equal P1 areas but old diffuse target below the new nodal floor fails on the same production helper. Artifact: `artifacts/A/ch14_reinit_sharp_volume_rca_CHK-RA-REINIT-SHARP-VOLUME-001.md`. No production source change yet; next step is a theory-first retraction contract decision, not scale-list widening. [SOLID-X] RCA/probe/docs only; no solver source touched, no tested implementation deleted, and no FD/WENO/PPE fallback, damping/CFL workaround, smoothing, curvature cap, benchmark branch, blanket projection, QP-as-physics path, or hidden DCCD/UCCD damper introduced. |

| session_CHK | CHK-RA-CPU-GPU-UTIL-001 DONE 2026-05-10 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„Äå„Ç∑„É≥„Ç∞„É´Âá¶ÁêÜ„ÉªCPUË≤ÝËç∑100%„Å´„Çà„ÇäGPUÂà©Áî®Áéá„Åå‰∏ä„Åå„Å£„Å¶„ÅÑ„Å™„ÅÑ„Å®Êé®Ê∏¨„Åô„Çã„ÄÇCPUÂá¶ÁêÜ„ÇíË§áÊï∞CPU„Å´ÂàÜÊï£„Åó„Å¶1„Ç≥„Ç¢„ÅÇ„Åü„Çä„ÅÆCPUË≤ÝËç∑„Çí‰∏ã„Åí„Çâ„Çå„Çã„Åã„ÄÅ‰ªÆË™¨„ÅåÊàêÁ´ã„Åô„Çã„ÅãÊ§úË®º„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/cpu_gpu_parallelism_hypothesis_CHK-RA-CPU-GPU-UTIL-001.md`; wiki `docs/wiki/code/WIKI-L-042.md`. Passive remote live-run evidence supports the observation that ch14 GPU runs can pin one CPU core: capillary PID showed one main thread at 99.7% CPU, `/proc` 5 s sample `4.98 s` CPU = `1.00` core-equivalent, while GPU SM samples were `43--81%` (`pmon` PID samples `60--62%`). Stack samples did not support CPU multiprocessing as the primary fix: active frames were in CuPy/CUDA/cuSPARSE orchestration (`spsm`, `spSM_analysis`, `coosort/tocsc`), FCCD matrix-free small-kernel launch paths, and occasional checkpoint manifest hashing, not independent host-only numerical loops. Equivalent threaded `code_fingerprint` hashing was slower than serial (`serial avg 8.81 ms`; 4 threads `19.70 ms`; 16 threads `23.86 ms`). Verdict: single-core saturation symptom is real, but distributing current CPU-side work across CPU cores is unlikely to raise GPU utilization or wall-time performance; better targets are checkpoint-frame/fingerprint caching or gating, cuSPARSE analysis/conversion reuse, FCCD kernel fusion, and the WIKI-T-060/WIKI-L-026 device-resident Krylov/line-preconditioner path. Validation: `git diff --check` PASS; targeted retrieval scan for `CHK-RA-CPU-GPU-UTIL-001|WIKI-L-042` PASS. [SOLID-X] diagnostic/docs/wiki only; no solver source, experiment contract, pressure/PPE route, capillary force, DCCD/FCCD/UCCD kernel, reinit physics, damping/CFL workaround, smoothing, curvature cap, benchmark branch, fallback, or tested implementation changed. |

| session_CHK | CHK-RA-COMMON-FLUX-REMAP-001 DONE 2026-05-10 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„Äåconservative_common_flux„Åß„ÅØÁïåÈù¢ËøΩË∑°„É™„Éû„ÉÉ„Éó„Åå„Åß„Åç„Å™„ÅÑ„ÄÇ„Åì„Çå„ÅØÂ§ßÂïèÈ°å„Åß„ÅÇ„Çã„ÄÇÊòØÊ≠£„ÇíÊ±Ç„ÇÄ„ÄÇÂïèÈ°å„ÅÆÂéüÂõÝ„ÇíÁâ©ÁêÜÂ≠¶„ÉªÊï∞Â≠¶„ÅÆÁêÜË´ñ„Å´Âü∫„Å•„ÅÑ„Å¶‰ªÆË™¨ËÄÉÊ°à„ÅóÊé®ÁêÜ‚Ä¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇRCA: chapter-14 canonical YAMLs had promoted `conservative_common_flux`, but static/oscillating/capillary/Rayleigh--Taylor use interface-following fitted grids (`grid_rebuild_freq=1`), while the common-flux route explicitly failed on `will_rebuild=True`. The deeper defect was theoretical: grid rebuild remapped `psi,u,v`, not the conservative state `(q,m,p)`; q-only reinit was also either fail-closed or silently overwritten by the pre-reinit transport ledger when projection recording was disabled. Implemented a conservative representation operator `R_h`: fitted-grid rebuild remaps `q` and each momentum component, restores `1^T V q` and `1^T V p_a` in the rebuilt metric, derives `rho(q)` and `u=p/rho`, lets the existing divergence reprojector act, then resynchronizes `p=rho u`; fixed-grid reinit is lifted as a velocity-preserving bundle retraction `m^R=V rho(q^R), p^R=m^R u^T` while keeping capillary work labelled to the transport endpoint. Added manufactured remap and two-step pipeline regressions; updated SP-AJ, WIKI-T-164, artifact `artifacts/A/ch14_common_flux_remap_rca_CHK-RA-COMMON-FLUX-REMAP-001.md`, and the stale rising-bubble `T_final` YAML test expectation. Verification: initial two-step probe reproduced failures in static/oscillating/capillary and pass for rising-bubble; after fix all five canonical ch14 YAMLs pass two-step probe; `git diff --check` PASS; local targeted tests PASS `20 passed`; remote-first wrapper expanded to full suite PASS `659 passed, 33 skipped in 43.33s`. [SOLID-D] conservative remap/reinit representation correction with tests/docs; no FD/WENO/PPE fallback, damping/CFL workaround, smoothing, curvature cap, benchmark branch, blanket projection, QP-as-physics path, hidden DCCD/UCCD damper, or tested implementation deletion introduced. |

The KKT unknowns are `(f,p,lambda)`, where `p` is pressure reaction and `lambda` is the physical no-slip wall reaction; stationarity is `M_f(f-f_dag)+D_h^T p+(B_h R_h)^T lambda=0`, with constraints `D_h f=0` and `B_h R_h f=0`. Correct publication is single-state: `f in K_h`, `u=R_h f`, `m=rho u`, next transport uses the same `f`; restart must verify `D_h f`, `B_h R_h f`, `u=R_h f`, and `m=rho u`. Rejected shortcuts: boundary-face zeroing only, nodal post-clamping while preserving faces, recomputing faces from clamped nodes without Hodge projection, penalty slip, CFL/damping/smoothing/curvature caps, and DCCD/UCCD hidden dampers. Zero-start branch tests at N=32x64 with runner-equivalent nonuniform grid initialization rejected intrinsic common-flux instability (`sigma=g=0` gives exactly zero speed/KE/divergence), rejected static circular capillary pressure-jump as primary (`sigma=0.072,g=0,t=0.002`, KE `5.127129596395177e-09`, volume drift `4.087663367513539e-13`), and showed normal `sigma=0.072,g=9.81,t=0.002` has tiny volume drift `5.6208536767079e-11` and face divergence `4.5054715513970223e-10` but large face/nodal mismatch `2.783239863665568e-02`. Decomposition proved the mismatch is entirely wall-localized (`interior=0`, wall=`2.7832398636655688e-02`). One-step diagnostics showed reconstructed wall speed nonzero while nodal wall speed is zero; toggling `face_no_slip_boundary_state` does not remove it because zeroing boundary faces does not make `reconstruct_nodes` satisfy nodal no-slip. Turning off canonical/preserved face state is rejected: it fails earlier at `t=8.90983124435474e-04` with `speed_linf=250.80621064154008`, `ppe_rhs_max=1.0710995280956676e+13`, and `div_u_max=1.5784475205854836e+06`. Identified root: boundary-constrained Hodge incompatibility where the solver preserves a divergence-free face state `f`, clamps reconstructed nodal velocity to `B R f`, publishes momentum from `rho B R f`, then transports the next step with `f`; this breaks the common-flux phase-space identity at walls. Artifact: `artifacts/A/ch14_rising_bubble_root_cause_hypotheses_CHK-RA-CH14-BUBBLE-RCA-BOUNDARY-HODGE-001.md`. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-CH14-CLOSED-PERWALL-ADJOINT-001 DONE 2026-05-08 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÁ∏¶ÊñπÂêë„Å´„ÅØÂ£Å„ÅÇ„Çä„ÄÅÊ®™ÊñπÂêë„ÅØÂë®ÊúüÂ¢ÉÁïå„Åß„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/ch14_closed_periodic_wall_adjoint_CHK-RA-CH14-CLOSED-PERWALL-ADJOINT-001.md`. Theory-first check: closed-interface capillary wave with horizontal periodicity and vertical walls is admissible on the constrained velocity space, but production must satisfy the same FCCD transport adjoint as `face_divergence`. The previous wall-axis `_negative_face_divergence_adjoint` included boundary nodal covectors, used a length-`N-1` weight on an `N+1` array, and had the opposite sign. Fixed the wall branch to zero boundary weights and return `weighted[1:] - weighted[:-1]`, i.e. the exact `(-D_f)^T` for wall `face_divergence`; added nonuniform `periodic_wall` regression proving `<eta, -D_f F> = <(-D_f)^T eta, F>` for both axes. Validation: remote wrapper PASS `633 passed, 33 skipped in 44.12s`; temporary N32/T1 closed perturbed-circle capillary-wave run with `{x: periodic, y: wall}` PASS (`99` steps, `t_final=1.0`, KE `7.309138e-10 -> 8.909943e-06`, volume drift max `1.114287e-06`, final speed Linf `1.300684e-03`, final `v(y=0/1)` Linf `0`, final x-periodic velocity mismatch `0`) and generated psi/velocity/pressure PDFs under `experiment/ch14/results/_tmp_ch14_closed_capillary_wave_n32_t1`. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-CH14-VARIATIONAL-PRESSURE-COMPLEX-001 DONE 2026-05-07 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„Äå„Åù„ÅÆÊñπÂêë„ÅßÁêÜË´ñ„ÇíÁ™Å„ÅçË©∞„ÇÅ„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/ch14_variational_pressure_complex_theory_CHK-RA-CH14-VARIATIONAL-PRESSURE-COMPLEX-001.md`. Theory closed around physical quotient spaces `(P_h,V_h)`, production divergence `D`, pressure metric `W`, kinetic face metric `M=Q_f/alpha_f`, and pressure reaction `G=M^{-1}D^TW` for subtractive projection convention. Main theorem: scalar PPE symmetry is insufficient; pressure API must satisfy `<Gp,w>_M=<p,Dw>_W`, `L=DG`, `<p,Lp>_W=||Gp||_M^2`, and any divergence-equivalent `G+Z, DZ=0, Z!=0` is a nonphysical pressure-work channel. Capillary/source shifts are affine terms `pressure_face_reaction(p;c)=Gp-c`; component and capillary release use `X=[G,B]`, `h=s-Xz`, `X^TMXz=X^TMs` so `D h=0`, `B^TMh=0`, and `dE[T h]=-||h||_M^2`. Existing scalar PPE reuse is valid only if `L_old=L_var` on the active quotient/coefficients; otherwise both scalar solve and face correction move to `L_var=DG`. Phase-separated zero-alpha faces must be removed from active face space or fail close. High-order FCCD must be an SBP/Riesz pair, not independent compact gradient as pressure force. Validation gates G0-G7 defined for quotient, Green identity, scalar identity, positivity, saddle, static constrained criticality, dynamic release, and reinit separation. [SOLID-X] theory/docs only; no production solver/config/result changed, no tested implementation deleted, no FD/WENO/PPE fallback, damping/CFL workaround, smoothing, curvature cap, benchmark branch, blanket projection, or QP-as-physics path introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-CH14-PRESSURE-ADJOINT-REMEDY-DECISION-001 DONE 2026-05-07 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÁêÜË´ñ„ÇíÁ™Å„ÅçË©∞„ÇÅ„Å¶„ÅÑ„Å£„Å¶„Å©„Å°„Çâ„Åå„ÅÑ„ÅÑ„ÅãÂà§Êñ≠ÊÝπÊãÝ„ÇíËÄÉ„Åà„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/ch14_pressure_adjoint_remedy_decision_CHK-RA-CH14-PRESSURE-ADJOINT-REMEDY-DECISION-001.md`. Decision theorem: for fixed production `(D_f,M_A,W_p)`, pressure reaction is uniquely `G_var=-M_A^{-1}D_f^TW_p` from kinetic-energy KKT; any `G_alt=G_var+Z, D_fZ=0, Z!=0` is a nonphysical pressure work channel unless a kinetic SPD metric is proven. Therefore the selected production remedy is not a capillary-only side corrector, but an FCCD variational pressure API redesign: `pressure_fluxes` must expose the Riesz-adjoint face representative, or non-adjoint compact gradients must be renamed/split as diagnostics. Reusing current scalar PPE is allowed only after proving `D_fG_FCCD=D_fG_var` with active quotient/coefficients; otherwise scalar PPE becomes `L_var=D_fG_var`. CCD/FCCD/UCCD compatibility and GPU-first criteria both favor a single variational pressure-face SSoT shared by projection, capillary Hodge, component saddles, diagnostics, and momentum coupling. [SOLID-X] theory/decision docs only; no production solver/config/result changed, no tested implementation deleted, no FD/WENO/PPE fallback, damping/CFL workaround, smoothing, curvature cap, benchmark branch, blanket projection, or QP-as-physics path introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-CH14-PRESSURE-ADJOINT-RCA-001 DONE 2026-05-07 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÊÆãË™≤È°å„Å´ÂØæ„Åó„ÄÅÂïèÈ°å„ÅÆÊÝπÊú¨ÂéüÂõÝ„ÇíÁâ©ÁêÜÂ≠¶„ÉªÊï∞Â≠¶„ÅÆÁêÜË´ñ„Å´Âü∫„Å•„ÅÑ„Å¶‰ªÆË™¨ËÄÉÊ°à„ÅóÊé®ÁêÜ„ÄÇ‰ªÆË™¨„Çí„Åß„Åç„Çã„ÅÝ„ÅëÂ§öÊï∞ÂâµÂá∫„Åó„ÄÅ„Åù„Çå„ÇíÊ§úË®º„Åô„Çã„Åì„Å®„ÅßÂïèÈ°åÁâπÂÆö„ÄÇÁêÜË´ñÁµ∂ÂØæË¶ñ„ÄÅÂ∞èÊâãÂÖà‰øÆÊ≠£Á¶ÅÊ≠¢„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/ch14_pressure_adjoint_residual_rca_CHK-RA-CH14-PRESSURE-ADJOINT-RCA-001.md`. Tested 15 hypotheses against the discrete constraint-reaction theorem `M_A G_A = -D_f^T W_p` on the physical periodic quotient. Reduced probes: FVM gives adjoint residual `0` to roundoff; FCCD gives symmetric scalar PPE `W_pD_fG_A` to roundoff but pressure-face adjoint residual `2.091171e-01` at N32 and nonzero best diagonal-metric residual `9.462458e-02`. Verdict: current FCCD `pressure_fluxes` returns a divergence-equivalent pressure representative `G_FCCD=G_var+Z` with `G_var=-M_A^{-1}D_f^TW_p`, `D_fZ=0`, `Z!=0`, so the PPE can satisfy `D_fu=0` while the face reaction is not kinetic-energy-adjoint. The reported `~4.45e-01` contract residual additionally contains a diagnostic artifact from unrestricted periodic image DOFs. Active object is `(q_c,D_f,G_A(q_c),M_A(q_c),T_f(q_c),S_h,V_m,h)` with `q_c=q_T`, `G_Ap=div_op.pressure_fluxes(p,rho_c,zero_jump_kwargs)`, and pressure-adjoint metric `<G_Ap,w>_{M_A}=<p,D_fw>_{W_p}`. If pressure adjointness fails, capillary Hodge energy theorem fails closed. Material coefficients must be from `q_T` or post-reinit `q^{n+1}` must pass endpoint-equivalence ledger. Primitive reaction law is coupled saddle `h=s-G_Ap-Bmu`, `D_fh=0`, `B^TM_Ah=0`; PPE-solve elimination uses `Z_A(c)=c-L_A(c)`, `C_ij=B_i^TM_AZ_A(B_j)`, `r_i=B_i^TM_AZ_A(s)`, `c_corrected=s-Bmu`. The earlier symmetric `Z_i^TM_AZ_j`/`B_hodge` shortcut is valid only after proving `range(G_A)` is `M_A`-orthogonal. Sign-power theorem becomes `<s,h>_{M_A}=||h||_{M_A}^2` and `dE[T_fh]=-||h||_{M_A}^2`. GPU geometry is theorem-level: production `dS_h,dV_h` must be the same P1 local formulas implemented with `xp` masks/regularity, while host-loop graph traversal is diagnostic unless proven identical. Corrector must receive the full endpoint-exact cochain; range projection remains diagnostic/reaction-only. Reinit/profile arrow `q_T -> q^{n+1}` is ledgered separately from capillary work. CCD/FCCD/UCCD coupling is preserved because `P_f,D_f,M_f`, pressure range, and projected face velocity are shared face-complex objects. Judgement: for the current LevelSet/CLS solver, use the conservative face-psi transport endpoint VJP as the production capillary cochain; trace-vertex endpoint is valid only after a trace-primary transport/state and CCD-family trace-map redesign. Evidence: both cochains are self-adjoint for their own endpoint, but trace force vs actual conservative endpoint has work residual `2.414e-01` at N32 circle and `2.709e-01` at N32 ellipse, while conservative endpoint work residual is roundoff (`~1e-16`). Streamfunction divergence-free face velocities still show `O(1)` relative mismatch between conservative-induced trace velocity and direct `C_K` trace velocity. CCD/FCCD/UCCD criterion strengthens the decision: conservative endpoint uses FCCD `face_value`/`face_divergence` and the same face-native projection complex that couples to UCCD6 momentum and CCD viscosity, whereas current trace candidate uses simple face-to-node reconstruction plus P1 trace and is not yet a compact/mimetic CCD-family trace map. Profile sensitivity supports trace-primary as a future representation concern but does not justify endpoint mixing. No production source/config/result changed. `git diff --check` PASS. Exact pinned projection reduces trace corrected Hodge norm by `2.44x..3.97x` but leaves nonzero residual (`2.017e-03` static, `4.625e-03` oscillating), so solve failure is a near-term contaminant, while actual transport VJP mismatch remains the deeper force-side cause. Production affine metric replacement barely changed exact Hodge norms (`2.017e-03 -> 2.044e-03` static; `4.625e-03 -> 4.621e-03` oscillating), so metric mismatch is theorem cleanup but not primary N32 driver. Reinit remains a separate representation defect (`Delta S_reinit~1.27e-02`, zero-level displacement `9.72e-03`, crossing changes `144`) but not the sole force-side cause. Recommended next step: first make trace Hodge projection solve gauge-fixed/robust and rerun N32/T1; then implement actual FCCD transport-endpoint VJP gate/cochain. Remote N32/T1 with visualization: static droplet final KE `5.284015e-09`, max volume drift `1.903440e-15`, deformation `0`, max snapshot velocity Linf `1.833331e-05`, max corrected Hodge weighted L2 `2.814614e-04`; oscillating droplet final KE `3.643971e-04`, max volume drift `2.428289e-15`, signed deformation `7.617534e-02 -> 4.334637e-02`, max snapshot velocity Linf `9.417805e-03`, max corrected Hodge weighted L2 `4.477470e-02`. Visualization generated in `experiment/ch14/results/_tmp_ch14_static_droplet_n32_t1_component_hodge/`, `experiment/ch14/results/_tmp_ch14_oscillating_droplet_n32_t1_component_hodge/`, plus summary PDF `experiment/ch14/results/ch14_component_hodge_n32_t1_summary.pdf`. Verdict: zero-drive bug is fixed for oscillating droplets; static droplet is volume/deformation stable but not roundoff static, so full fixed-stratum Riesz cochain `s=-M_f^{-1}T^Td(sigma S_h)^T` remains the next theorem-grade obligation. Main merge not performed. [SOLID-X] pressure-stage face-cochain responsibility isolated; no tested implementation deleted; no FD/WENO/PPE fallback, damping/CFL workaround, curvature cap, smoothing, benchmark-name branch, blanket `c -> Pi_R c`, or QP-as-physics path introduced. |

Main finding: static-circle residual is not curvature noise, sign convention, fitted-grid artifact, resolution-only error, missing continuum volume multiplier, or generic PPE failure. Exact constant curvature `kappa=¬±1/R` leaves the same O(4-5%) weighted Hodge residual; P2 surface covector plus `¬±lambda dV_h` remains invalid; wall-bounded flat constant jumps pass to `~1.4e-9` relative residual, proving affine jump/FCCD range can be exact in compatible topology. Closed geometry tests show square/diamond residuals smaller while circle retains O(4-5%); nodal phase-step pressure candidates mismatch the local circular cut-face cochain by ~20%. Verdict: direct no-oscillation cause remains production `c -> Pi_range(c)`; deeper blocker is closed-interface cochain-complex mismatch `local cut-face B_Gamma(j) notin range(A_f G_f)` for curved closed interfaces. Next constructive target: a closed-interface exact capillary cochain that gives `P_h c‚âà0` for constant Young-Laplace circle and `P_h c!=0` for noncritical modes, in the same `(D_f,A_f,G_f)` complex. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-CAP-REINIT-HODGE-THEORY-001 DONE 2026-05-06 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„Äå„Åß„ÅØ„ÄÅËÄÉÂØü„Çí„Åï„Çâ„Å´Èáç„Å≠„ÄÅÁêÜË´ñ„ÇíÂÆåÊàê„Åï„Åõ„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/capillary_reinit_hodge_complete_theory_CHK-RA-CAP-REINIT-HODGE-THEORY-001.md`. Completed the generic theory as a split-map variational contract: `q^n --T_h(u_f)--> q_T --Pi_h--> q^{n+1}`. `T_h` is physical projection-native interface transport; `Pi_h` is Ridge-Eikonal/profile metric projection. Capillary work pairs only with the velocity-generated transport displacement `delta q_T`, unless `Pi_h` is explicitly modeled and charged as metric/topology dissipation. Fixed-topology production contract: `Gamma(q^{n+1})=Gamma(q_T)` and `S_h(Gamma(q^{n+1}))=S_h(Gamma(q_T))`; otherwise `Delta S_Pi` is projection defect, not capillary motion. The physical cochain is `T_h^* dS_h`, then the same weighted face-space Hodge gate decides both static and dynamic behavior: static iff `P_h c_sigma=0`, dynamic release iff `P_h c_sigma!=0`. Established theorems: gauge-invariant reinit, projection defect not capillary force, static/dynamic separation survives identity-preserving reinit, and range replacement remains invalid. Current implementation risk identified: `state.psi_previous -> state.psi` may be an unlabelled transport+reinit endpoint, while the completed theory requires separate `psi_before_transport`, `psi_after_transport_before_reinit`, and `psi_after_reinit`. Required gates: state split, projection identity, virtual work, static Hodge, dynamic release, reinit defect, energy ledger. [SOLID-X] theory/docs only; no solver/config production change; no tested implementation deleted; no FD/WENO/PPE fallback or alternate route introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-CAP-STATIC-DYNAMIC-GATE-001 DONE 2026-05-06 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÊåØÂãïÊ∂≤Êª¥Á≥ª„ÅßÈùôÊ≠¢„Åó„Å¶„Åó„Åæ„Çè„Å™„ÅÑ„Åã„ÄÅ‰∏ÄÊñπ„ÅßÈùôÊ≠¢Ê∂≤Êª¥Á≥ª„Å™„ÇâÂùáË°°„Åô„Çã„Åã„ÄÅÁêÜË´ñÁöÑ„Å´ÂæπÂ∫ïÁöÑ„Å´Ê§úË®º„Åó„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/capillary_static_dynamic_gate_verification_CHK-RA-CAP-STATIC-DYNAMIC-GATE-001.md`. Established the single generic gate: static equilibrium iff `P_h c_h=0`, dynamic release iff `P_h c_h!=0`, where `c_h` is the physical surface-energy cochain and `P_h` is the same weighted face-space Hodge projection used by PPE/corrector. Continuum checks: area-normalized radial modes have `dL/deps@0=0` but positive second variation, and finite perturbations have nonzero restoring derivative; ch14 ellipse (`a=0.275`, `b=0.225`, `s=0.1003353477`) has `dL/ds=2.353246e-01` and curvature span `2.456892e+00`, so it is not a static Young--Laplace equilibrium. Weighted finite-dimensional Hodge checks separated pure pressure-range static force (`||h||_M=4.87e-15`) from dynamic Hodge force (`||h||_M=3.57`, positive first-step KE coefficient) while showing `c -> Pi_R c` kills both. Existing ch14 evidence matches the warning: range-projected static N32/T4 is operationally quiet, but N32/T1 oscillating droplet is also machine-zero velocity; therefore zero velocity under deletion is not a variational static proof. Required gates: static Young--Laplace `||P_h c_h||` small on physical cochain, dynamic release `||P_h c_h||` nonzero with `K^1 ~ dt^2||P_h c_h||^2/2`, and reinit/remap separation. [SOLID-X] theory/verification/docs only; no solver/config production change; no tested implementation deleted; no FD/WENO/PPE fallback or alternate route introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-CAP-HODGE-PITFALL-001 DONE 2026-05-06 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåËêΩ„Å®„ÅóÁ©¥„Åå„Å™„ÅÑ„ÅãÂæπÂ∫ïÁöÑ„Å´ËÄÉ„Åà„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/capillary_variational_hodge_pitfalls_CHK-RA-CAP-HODGE-PITFALL-001.md`. Strict audit found theory survives only with tightened contracts. Critical pitfalls: (P1) `P_h` must use the physical face kinetic-energy/mass inner product and the exact PPE/corrector adjoint, not Euclidean `D^T`; toy weighted model showed a naive projection can match `D c` while being outside the correct pressure range by Wf-distance `9.523e-01`. (P2) distinguish force covector from acceleration cochain; virtual work pairs force with velocity, while corrector uses mass-inverted acceleration. (P3) full pressure vs pressure increment/history must name the unknown; using full Young--Laplace jump with an increment solve double-counts or kills force. (P4) raw curvature jump may contain numerical Hodge artifact; preserving full cochain is valid only after variational virtual-work verification. (P5) reinitialization/remap/grid rebuild are representation operations, not physical capillary relaxation, and must be excluded or explicitly represented in the transport map. (P6) boundary/contact/multi-component/body-force constraints change the admissible velocity space and equilibrium condition; no circle/static branch. (P7) topology changes and threshold degeneracy can make `E_h` nonsmooth. (P8) face/nodal reconstruction and CLS transport must use the same face velocity that appears in capillary work. Required before implementation: define mass inner product, unknown-specific jump, variational cochain builder, virtual-work gate, static critical-point gate, dynamic release gate, reinit separation gate. [SOLID-X] pitfall/docs only; no solver/config production change; no tested implementation deleted; no fallback/alternate route introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-CAP-HODGE-VERIFY-001 DONE 2026-05-06 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÁêÜË´ñ„ÅÆÊ≠£„Åó„Åï„ÇíÊ§úË®º„Åó„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/capillary_variational_hodge_theory_verification_CHK-RA-CAP-HODGE-VERIFY-001.md`. Verification layers: (1) continuum radial-mode finite-difference check on `r(theta)=R+eps cos(n theta)` confirmed first variation of perimeter is zero at the circle for volume-preserving modes `n>=1`, while finite-amplitude `n=2` perturbations have nonzero perimeter derivative (`dL/deps=2.512e-01,6.268e-01,1.244e+00` for eps `0.02,0.05,0.10`), supporting shape-agnostic critical-point logic; (2) finite-dimensional Hodge model with random `D`, SPD `A`, `G=D^T` confirmed `c=Pi_R c+h`, `D h‚âà0`, full corrector keeps `||h||` acceleration, while replacing `c` by `Pi_R c` gives exactly zero acceleration even for pure divergence-free cochains; (3) repo one-step N32 probe reproduced RCA: `range_projected` has nonzero jump/residual (`capillary_jump_linf=3.477644e-02`, `hodge=1.813600e-03`) but zero applied face/velocity, while `none` preserves applied face acceleration `1.813600e-03` and yields nonzero velocities (`u_linf=1.235913e-05`, `v_linf=9.806495e-06`). Verdict: theory is correct under its stated assumptions: `c_f` must be the transport-adjoint/discrete-gradient surface-energy covector and PPE/corrector must share the same face complex. Important limitation: this verifies the Hodge/projection contract, not the existing raw curvature cochain as a faithful variational cochain. [SOLID-X] verification/docs only; no solver/config production change; no tested implementation deleted; no FD/WENO/PPE fallback or alternate route introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-CAP-HODGE-THEORY-001 DONE 2026-05-06 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÁêÜË´ñÁ¢∫Á´ã„ÄÇÁâπÊÆäÁä∂Ê≥Å„Å´„Åó„Åã‰Ωø„Åà„Å™„ÅÑ„É≠„Ç∏„ÉÉ„ÇØ„ÅØÁ¶ÅÊ≠¢„ÄÇÊ±éÁî®ÁöÑ„Å´‰Ωø„Åà„Çã„Çà„ÅÜ„Å´„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/capillary_variational_hodge_theory_CHK-RA-CAP-HODGE-THEORY-001.md`. Established generic theorem: in the face-space projection complex, a capillary cochain `c_f` must enter the PPE/corrector as a force covector; solving `D_f A_f G_f p = D_f c_f + r_h` makes the post-correction acceleration `a_f=A_fG_fp-c_f` the discrete Leray/Hodge component that can change incompressible kinetic energy. Replacing `c_f` by `Pi_R c_f` in the corrector is a theorem-level dynamic kill switch for zero-predictor capillary release, because `p=pi_h` gives `a_f=0` for every geometry, not just static equilibria. Generic production contract: range projection is diagnostic decomposition only; production capillary cochain must be built as a transport-adjoint/discrete-gradient derivative of surface energy, and static rest is accepted only when the variational Hodge residual vanishes under the same face-velocity space. Equilibrium criterion is shape-agnostic: `P_h c_f=0` iff the discrete first variation of `E_h=sigma|Gamma_h|` is zero on all divergence-free admissible velocities (continuum constant generalized curvature per connected component, modified by body potentials/contact constraints), not `if circle/static`. Banned logic: shape-name branches, static/oscillating switches, blanket Hodge deletion, damping/CFL/curvature-cap/smoothing fixes, FD/WENO/PPE fallback substitutions. [SOLID-X] theory/docs only; no solver/config production change; no tested implementation deleted; no alternate numerical route introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-CH14-OSC-ZERO-DRIVE-001 DONE 2026-05-06 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÊåØÂãïÊ∂≤Êª¥„Å™„ÅÆ„Å´ÊåØÂãï„ÅåÁîü„Åò„Å™„ÅÑ„ÄÇÂïèÈ°å„ÅÆÂéüÂõÝ„ÇíÁâ©ÁêÜÂ≠¶„ÉªÊï∞Â≠¶„ÅÆÁêÜË´ñ„Å´Âü∫„Å•„ÅÑ„Å¶‰ªÆË™¨ËÄÉÊ°à„ÅóÊé®ÁêÜ„ÄÇ‰ªÆË™¨„Çí„Åß„Åç„Çã„ÅÝ„ÅëÂ§öÊï∞ÂâµÂá∫„Åó„ÄÅ„Åù„Çå„ÇíÊ§úË®º„Åô„Çã„Åì„Å®„ÅßÂïèÈ°åÁâπÂÆö„ÄÇÁêÜË´ñÁµ∂ÂØæË¶ñ„ÄÅÂ∞èÊâãÂÖà‰øÆÊ≠£Á¶ÅÊ≠¢„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇArtifact `artifacts/A/ch14_oscillating_droplet_zero_drive_rca_CHK-RA-CH14-OSC-ZERO-DRIVE-001.md`. Theory: deformed inviscid/capillary droplet at rest must develop nonzero pressure-gradient acceleration unless the Young--Laplace jump is an exact static equilibrium; only a circle has constant curvature static balance. RCA created 12 hypotheses and tested the dominant ones by local one-step algebraic probes plus remote in-memory N32/T0.1 four-way controls. Main finding: initial ellipse and nonzero curvature/jump exist (`kappa_max=40.856`, `capillary_jump_linf=3.477644e-02`), PPE DC converges, but `capillary_range_projection: range_projected` replaces the original jump cochain by its range projection in both pressure history and corrector, making the actually used face acceleration exactly zero (`capillary_face_linf=0`, stored pressure faces `0`, `u=v=0`). With diagnostic `capillary_range_projection:none`, the same initial state gives nonzero face acceleration (`1.813600e-03`) and nonzero first-step velocities (`u_linf=1.235913e-05`, `v_linf=9.806495e-06`), proving the projection/corrector selection is the zero-drive mechanism. Secondary finding: every-step Ridge--Eikonal reinitialization changes signed deformation even with zero velocity (`0.076175 -> 0.045033` in one step; remote T0.1 `0.076175 -> 0.044504` with KE=0), so the observed D(t) decay is not physical oscillation. Remote T0.1 controls: range+reinit0 keeps D nearly constant and KE zero; none+reinit0 produces KE `3.198901e-07` and snapshot velocity Linf `1.309306e-04`; none+reinit1 produces larger nonzero KE but D dominated by reinit. Verdict: root cause is an overgeneralized static-equilibrium range-projection closure plus a diagnostic-confounding reinit schedule; not missing initial velocity, viscosity overdamping, CFL, pressure-solver residual, absent curvature, or face reconstruction. No fix applied yet by user policy. [SOLID-X] RCA/docs only; no solver/config production change; no tested implementation deleted; no FD/WENO/PPE fallback or alternate scheme introduced. |

Fix: added output-only `pressure_bulk` snapshot renderer that masks the undefined diffuse interface layer (no smoothing/capping/damping) and updated N64 alpha-2 configs to use `pressure_bulk_t*.pdf`; raw scalar remains stored in `fields/pressure` for diagnostics. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-OSC-N64-029 DONE 2026-05-05 ‚Äî User observation„ÄåÂúßÂäõ„ÅåÁïåÈù¢‰ªòËøë„ÅßÂ§â„Å™ÊåØÂãï„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇRCA: affine pressure-jump IPC was solving with a previous face-pressure history in the predictor but then reusing the new full affine jump cochain as the velocity correction and next history; this violates the split PPE unknown/jump contract (`œá=Œ¥p` needs `Œ¥j`, or full-pressure solve needs source `D_f a_p^n` and correction `a_p^{n+1}-a_p^n`). Validation: local py_compile PASS; targeted affine/face-native and interface-stress tests PASS; remote GPU T=0.25 PASS final/max KE `1.440847e-05`, max volume drift `5.444021e-07`; remote GPU T=1 PASS final/max KE `9.702873e-05`, max volume drift `9.394296e-06` (old T=1 max KE `2.942622e-04`); remote GPU T=2.5 PASS steps `4047`, final/max KE `3.232768e-04`/`3.232768e-04`, no KE>=`1e-3`, max volume drift `6.767031e-05`, signed deformation `0.0922667 -> 0.0853105`, pressure contrast `0.252626 -> 0.229567` with `|Œîp|` never reaching `0.5` (old T=2.5 max KE `2.073979e-03`, `|Œîp|` spike `8.626156`, KE>=`1e-3` at t=`2.441153`). Face pressure acceleration at T=2.5 stays O(`1e-2`) instead of old O(`10--50`). Remaining diagnostic nuance: nodal values inside the diffuse interface band are phase-extension/gauge data and can show undershoot, while bulk phase pressure and the face cochain used by momentum are now stable; one-sided/Hodge pressure visualization remains a separate output-quality task. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-OSC-N64-027 DONE 2026-05-05 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÁêÜË´ñ„ÇíÁ™Å„ÅçË©∞„ÇÅ„Å¶„ÄÅÊÆãË™≤È°å„Å´ÂØæÂá¶„Åó„Å¶„ÄÇËß£Ê±∫„ÅÆÁ≥∏Âè£„ÅåË¶ã„Åà„Çã„Åæ„Åß„ÄÅ„ÇÇ„Åó„Åè„ÅØ15ÂàÜÁµåÈÅé„Åô„Çã„Åæ„ÅßÊÄùËÄÉ„ÇíÁ∂ö„Åë„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇTheory audit narrowed residuals to two mathematically concrete defects: (1) `transport_variational_p2_ale_discrete_gradient` uses `previous_surface_energy` only as the pre-remap endpoint of one rebuild step, but the field was not cleared on non-rebuild steps, so stale old-grid energy could contaminate later Gonzalez corrections; fixed by making it step-local in `_advance_interface_stage` and added regression `test_ale_discrete_gradient_previous_surface_energy_is_step_local`. (2) `pressure_contrast`/`laplace_pressure` diagnostics used arithmetic node means on non-uniform fitted grids; corrected to volume-weighted phase means (`‚à´p dV/‚à´dV`) and added non-uniform diagnostic regression. Evidence: existing T20/one-period data show real KE blow-up (`KE>=1e-3` at t‚âà2.44/3.76, catastrophic at t‚âà10.756/19.177) while volume drift remains small, so remaining root target is not volume loss but capillary face-work/Hodge representative or transport-remap adjoint closure. Validation: local `py_compile` PASS; `test_diagnostics.py`: 17 passed; `test_ns_pipeline_fccd.py -k ale_discrete_gradient_previous_surface_energy_is_step_local or affine_jump or phase_separated`: 18 passed. [SOLID-X] SRP preserved; no tested code deleted; no damping/CFL tuning/smoothing/curvature cap workaround introduced. |
| session_CHK | CHK-RA-OSC-N64-026 DONE 2026-05-05 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„Äå„Åò„ÇÉ„ÅÇÊó©ÈÄüÂèñ„ÇäÊéõ„Åã„Å£„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇIdentified the affine pressure-history error behind misleading/damping-looking bubble pressure: the face-native `affine_jump` path must not re-evaluate pressure history from the accumulated scalar representative because `G_Œì(p;B)=G(p)-B` is affine in the jump cochain. Changed affine face-native IPC so the next pressure-history state is the exact face cochain used by the velocity corrector, while the scalar pressure representative is not accumulated on this route. Added `pressure_correction_face_components` as an operation-local step state and reused it in the corrector to avoid duplicate GPU face-gradient work. Added GPU-batched `pressure_contrast=<p>_liquid-<p>_gas` diagnostic and enabled it in the N64 alpha-2 one-period YAML with `pressure_contrast.pdf`. Baseline remote rerun reproduced current paper values: V10-a `N=64` centroid `1.145e-02`, shape L1 `1.457e-02`, mass drift `2.811e-04`; `N=128` centroid `4.911e-03`, shape L1 `8.307e-03`, mass drift `4.703e-06`; V10-b `N=128,T=8` reversal L1 `2.248e-02`, mass drift `4.281e-04`. Added diagnostic `experiment/ch13/diagnose_V10_shape_error.py`; remote diagnostic tested 18 variants across slot/circle/wide-slot/no-mass-correction/mass-correction-every-step/CFL/eps and single-vortex T/reinit/mass/CFL/N controls. Verdict: V10-a shape error is fixed-grid steep-CLS spatial phase/threshold geometry plus slot/corner under-resolution; slot doubles shape L1 (`8.307e-03` vs smooth circle `3.877e-03`) but centroid is not slot-only (`circle_N128` centroid `5.461e-03`). V10-b error is grid-scale folded-filament irreversibility; N64 T sweep width proxy/h `6.19‚Üí3.81‚Üí2.01‚Üí1.62` while L1 `1.672e-03‚Üí2.227e-03‚Üí1.213e-02‚Üí5.013e-02`; no-reinit, reinit-every-step, mass-off, and CFL-half do not provide a theory-valid fix. Added artifact `artifacts/A/review_CHK-RA-CH13-V10-SHAPE-001.md`; updated `paper/sections/13_verification.tex`, `13e_nonuniform_ns.tex`, `13f_error_budget.tex`, and `15_conclusion.tex` to replace slot-only / literal filament<h wording with measured fixed-grid phase/threshold + slot and grid-scale folded-filament Type-D framing. Validation: `py_compile` PASS; `git diff --check` PASS; baseline remote V10 PASS; diagnostic remote PASS; `make -C paper` PASS (`paper/main.pdf`, 244 pp; remaining diagnostics are existing `sections/09f_pressure_summary.tex:57` underfull and Chapter 12 float-only page warning). Verdict: V7 ‰ΩéÊ¨°Êï∞„ÅÆ‰∏ªÂõÝ„ÅØ IMEX-BDF2 ‰øÇÊï∞„ÇÑÂÜçÂàùÊúüÂåñ cadence „Åß„ÅØ„Å™„ÅèÔºåcapillary pressure-jump / affine FCCD projection „Åå $\psi\simeq0.5$ „ÅÆÁïåÈù¢Â∏Ø„Åß‰Ωú„Çã‰ΩéÊ≠£ÂâáÊÄß„ÉªÂàÜË£ÇË™§Â∑Æ„ÄÇBaseline V7 remote rerun reproduced slope `1.48`; diagnostic `experiment/ch13/diagnose_V7_imex_bdf2_order.py` with ref128 tested `each_step` (slope `1.59`, n64 err `3.666e-03`), `fixed_reinit_count` (`1.50`, `9.358e-04`), `no_reinit` (`1.57`, `1.670e-03`), `static_interface` (`1.58`, `1.870e-03`), `static_no_capillary` (`1.54`, `6.831e-08`), `uniform_static_capillary` (`1.59`, `8.198e-03`), and `uniform_static_no_capillary` (`1.58`, `7.302e-08`). Interpretation: reinit/reference affect constants; interface transport Lie split is not necessary; capillary jump is sufficient and sigma removal collapses amplitude by 4--5 orders; density/viscosity jumps are not primary. Added artifact `artifacts/A/review_CHK-RA-CH13-V7-IMEX-001.md`; updated `paper/sections/00_abstract.tex`, `01_introduction.tex`, `13_verification.tex`, `13d_density_ratio.tex`, `13f_error_budget.tex`, and `15_conclusion.tex` from Lie/reinit framing to capillary pressure-jump/projection Type-D framing. Findings: active CH13 scripts contain no retired CCD-LU PPE, LGMRES/BiCGSTAB PPE, WENO production path, or stale CN/ADI framing; V6/V7/V9 use current ¬ß14 stack subset. Refresh: `make run-all CH=ch13` PASS + `make pull` PASS; V5--V10 paper numbers updated, V7 coupled-stack slope corrected to $1.04$ Lie-limited, V8 $\alpha=2,N=96$ pressure error to $2.86\%$, V10-a mass drift to $4.70\times10^{-4}\%$, and V10-b mass/L1 to $0.0428\%$ / $2.248\times10^{-2}$. V9 old local-$\varepsilon$ superiority claim was invalid under current direct-$\psi$/HFE short probe because B/C are identical; paper and `experiment/ch13/exp_V9_local_eps_nonuniform.py` now frame it as nominal/local switch stability diagnostic, and V9 figures were regenerated. Audit confirmed Appendix C.2.1 ghost-cell handling is an alternative only; standard library wall CCD uses the paper-exact boundary-scheme method with unique `alpha=3/2`, six-point `O(h^4)` Eq-II, and fail-closed no-fallback behavior. Appendix C.2.2 periodic CCD matches the paper: `2N x 2N` block-circulant LU over unique nodes, wrapped RHS, and node `N` synced to node `0`. Research memo corrections were recorded: endpoint-value/first-derivative cubic Hermite face interpolation is not the paper FCCD `D1 u - D2 q` operator; split-PPE/GFM/HFE ghost jets are Chapter 9/11 interface closure, not C.2 domain-boundary ghost cells; free-`alpha` wall tuning is a different nonstandard scheme. Changes: added Appendix C.2 scope warning in `paper/sections/appendix_ccd_impl_s1.tex`; added CPU periodic CCD topology/accuracy test; added GPU periodic CCD parity/device-residency smoke. Artifact: `artifacts/A/review_CHK-RA-APPC2-IMPL-001.md`. Audit found Chapter 11 stage order/material/curvature/FCCD-affine projection largely paper-consistent, but two implementation divergences remained: direct-œà transport lacked the adaptive `M/M_ref > Œ∏_reinit` trigger, and the modern NS pipeline treated PPE pressure as a total/warm-start field instead of Chapter 11 IPC increment. Fixes: config/runtime/direct transport now support adaptive reinit default with threshold `1.10` while explicit `every_steps` schedules stay fixed; IPC now feeds previous total pressure into predictor as `-rho^-1 grad(p^n)`, solves PPE for `delta p` from zero, corrects velocity with `delta p`, and stores total pressure `p^n+delta p` with separate base pressure tracking. Audit found Chapter 10 composite monitor, CCD metric transform, non-uniform FCCD central face, Ridge--Eikonal D1/D3/D4, wall-axis closure, and GPU FMM accepted-set path paper-consistent, but two divergences remained: public/direct non-uniform solver defaults still rebuilt the grid every step, and RidgeExtractor used a low-order physical Hessian on non-uniform grids. Chapter 7 viscous Helmholtz DC conforms to high-order residual fixed point `A_H=I-tau L_{nu,H}` with low-order Helmholtz `A_L` sharing `mu`, `rho`, boundary topology, and physical nonuniform coordinates. Chapter 9 PPE DC conforms to `L_H` residual / distinct low-order `L_L` correction, affine jump RHS single application, and FD direct low-order factor reuse. Found and fixed one true nonuniform-grid defect: periodic nonuniform FCCD face divergence and low-order FD periodic wrap rows used face/uniform widths instead of periodic nodal control-volume widths; fixed in `src/twophase/ccd/fccd.py` and `src/twophase/ppe/ppe_builder_helpers.py` with regression tests. Chapter 7 viscous Helmholtz DC is adopted as high-residual fixed-point solve with low-order Helmholtz `A_L` sharing `mu`, `rho`, boundary topology, and interface-band structure; automatic GMRES/FGMRES fallback is not adopted because current reproducible contract keeps GMRES as explicit YAML-selected comparison path and no new stagnation benchmark was provided. Chapter 9 PPE DC is adopted for phase-separated PPE + HFE + phase-internal constant-density Poisson with `DC k=3` and high-residual monitoring; fixed-count DC is not promoted to primary high-density smoothed-Heaviside one-field PPE. PPE GMRES/FGMRES routing is not adopted under current PR-6. Artifact: `artifacts/A/review_CHK-RA-DC-ADOPT-001.md`. Main oscillating-droplet YAML now uses a true ellipse (`semi_axes=[0.055,0.045]`, signed deformation target `D0=0.10`), grid monitor `alpha=4` on both fitted axes, dynamic rebuild `schedule=1`, water-air viscosities/densities, viscous `implicit_bdf2` with DC, and Rayleigh-Lamb period-one scaling via `sigma=0.811133` (`R_area=sqrt(ab)=0.04975`, `omega=6.283185`, `T_period‚âà1.00000007`). Theory-fidelity fixes: ellipse SDF changed to first-order distance approximation `F/|‚àáF|` near the interface; curvature cap raised to `40` to exceed the true ellipse curvature max `‚âà27.2`; reinitialization kept disabled after small-droplet reinit smoke showed severe volume loss. Validation: targeted `shape_from_dict_ellipse` PASS; runtime parse confirms `alpha=(4,4)`, rebuild `1`, CFL capillary factor `0.01`, predictor `ImplicitBDF2ViscousPredictor`. Remote smoke evidence: strong-small-ellipse variants failed early; final larger period-one ellipse with CFL 1.0 failed at `t=0.01765`, while same physics with `cfl=0.2` reached `t=0.02` (`1004` samples) with `KE=3.674e-05`, volume drift `4.936e-08`, signed deformation `0.06951 -> 0.06918`. Full `T_final=1.5` period-scale run not executed to completion because stable CFL implies ~7.5e4 steps under `N=128, alpha=4, schedule=1, DC`. [SOLID-X] no SOLID violation found; shape deserialization is isolated in initial-condition primitives/factory; solver/DC boundaries unchanged. |

RCA: static-droplet GPU stack had avoidable host synchronization and intermittent CPU/device stalls from per-step full-field metric copies, always-on step diagnostics, dynamic nonuniform remap for an equilibrium droplet, per-step CFL max-speed reductions, and repeated device face-spacing materialization in FCCD projection. Fixes: CH13 ¬ß14-stack helper and V9 now collect `u_inf`, volume drift, and pressure contrast as device reductions with one scalar transfer per step and keep full fields on device until final output; default static helper debug diagnostics are off unless requested. Added checked-in `experiment/ch14/config/ch14_static_droplet_periodic.yaml` for the config-driven static droplet route with fully periodic boundaries, initially fitted static nonuniform grid (`schedule: static`), fixed capillary-limit `dt=0.00988`, and step diagnostics off. Runner wording now treats `circle` as circle-interface benchmarks, not only rising bubble. FCCD face-flux projection now caches FVM face spacings and avoids allocating/copying them at all on the `pressure_gradient=fccd` route. Validation: local py_compile PASS for touched Python files; `git diff --check` PASS; remote `make cycle EXP=experiment/ch13/exp_V6_density_ratio_convergence.py` PASS; remote `make cycle EXP=experiment/run.py ARGS="--config ch14_static_droplet_periodic"` PASS before and after fixed-dt/cache change (`T=1.0`, 102 steps, final `KE‚âà6.035e-05`, static nonuniform grid logged); remote targeted projection tests PASS (`test_fccd_projection_divergence_matches_ppe_operator_nonuniform_wall`, `test_phase_separated_fccd_projection_matches_ppe_operator_nonuniform_wall`). Broad `make test -k fccd`/node-id attempts also ran unrelated/full suites because of Makefile argument behavior and failed on pre-existing/mismatched ch13 config, local-epsilon, tiny-grid interface-stress, ridge/reinit thresholds; not treated as regressions from this patch. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-VISCOUS-DC-003 DONE 2026-05-02 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„Äå‰ªÆË™¨Ê§úË®º„ÇíË°å„Å™„Å£„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇValidated CHK-RA-VISCOUS-DC-002 hypotheses with targeted local and remote experiments. Pattern fixedness: CSC `indptr/indices` stayed identical under `mu/rho` changes and under coordinate changes after nonuniform grid rebuild (`coords-differ=True`), proving fixed sparse pattern is valid when shape/topology are unchanged while numeric factor caching remains unsafe for dynamic grids. Remote GPU N=128 alpha=4 low-Helmholtz timing: init `0.6723s`; matrix build `0.2487+0.2447s`; sparse LU `0.0944+0.0843s`; two-component low solve mean `0.1560s`; direct device factor solve mean `0.1513s`; measured `asnumpy` total only `0.00050s`, correcting prior H4 from raw transfer bottleneck to Python/host sparse-assembly bottleneck. Inexact low solve test: CPU N=16 variable-coefficient high-residual DC with low GMRES tolerances `1e-2..1e-8` matched LU residual contraction (`3.076e-02 -> ~4.91e-03`, ratio `~1.60e-01`), rejecting ‚Äúsparse LU is required for accuracy‚Äù as a theorem. Scalar-low test: common `A_S=0.5(A_x+A_y)` / `c=3/2` contracted high residual better in the representative case (`2.991e-02 -> 3.696e-03`, ratio `1.236e-01`) with random Rayleigh component/scalar ratios `0.951..1.048`, validating theory-first scalar Helmholtz design. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-VISCOUS-DC-002 DONE 2026-05-02 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåÂïèÈ°å„ÅÆÂéüÂõÝ„ÇíÁâ©ÁêÜÂ≠¶„ÉªÊï∞Â≠¶„ÅÆÁêÜË´ñ„Å´Âü∫„Å•„ÅÑ„Å¶‰ªÆË™¨ËÄÉÊ°à„ÅóÊé®ÁêÜ„Äç„ÄåÂõÝÂ≠êÂåñ„Ç≠„É£„ÉÉ„Ç∑„É•„ÅØÊÇ™Êâã„Äç„ÄåÁñéË°åÂàó„Éë„Çø„Éº„É≥Âõ∫ÂÆöÂåñ„ÅØ„Åæ„Åö„Éà„É©„Ç§„Äç„Äåmatrix-free low solve „ÅÆÁ≤æÂ∫¶Êá∏Âøµ„Äç„ÄåÂÖ±ÈÄö scalar Helmholtz Ëøë‰ºº„ÅØÁêÜË´ñÁ¢∫Á´ã„Åã„Çâ„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇN=128/T=1/alpha=4 fully-periodic static droplet validation completed on remote GPU with implicit-BDF2 viscous `defect_correction`: 810 steps to `t=1.0`, wall time 28m04s, final/max KE `3.2082084486958047e-06`, volume drift `0.0`, deformation `0.0`. RCA: this YAML is static-grid, not per-step grid rebuild (`_rebuild_freq=0`); low-order Helmholtz micro-timing on the same fitted N=128 grid gave sparse-matrix build `0.253+0.247s`, sparse LU `0.0868+0.0876s`, and one two-component triangular solve `0.146s`, so current DC low path costs roughly `1.26s/step` from Python/host assembly, two component LU factors, and repeated low solves. Root cause identified as mathematical-design mismatch: structured tensor-grid Helmholtz correction is implemented as fresh general sparse host-built problem each step. Rejected: grid rebuild as current-run cause, LU as sole bottleneck, matrix-free inaccuracy as theorem, common scalar low operator impossibility. Supported: fixed row/column sparse pattern with GPU-side value update, GPU-resident RHS/correction, and theory-first scalar Helmholtz low operator (`c=3/2` spectrally equivalent to component stress weights) as next design directions. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-VISCOUS-DC-001 DONE 2026-05-02 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåResearchArchitect„Äç„Äåimplicit BDF2 Á≤òÊÄßÈÝÖ„Çí GMRES „Åß„ÅØ„Å™„Åè DC „ÅßËß£„ÅèÁêÜË´ñÊßãÁØâ„ÉªÂÆüË£ÖË®≠Ë®à„ÉªÊ§úË®º„Äç„ÄåGMRES „ÅØ fallback „Åß„ÅØ„Å™„Åè YAML ÈÅ∏ÊäûÂà∂„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇNew worktree `.claude/worktrees/ra-viscous-dc-20260502` / branch `ra-viscous-dc-20260502` from `main`; lock `docs/locks/ra-viscous-dc-20260502.lock.json` acquired. Theory: the BDF2 time integrator is unchanged; each step solves the high-order viscous Helmholtz equation `A_H u^*=b` by residual defect correction with low-order `A_L`, preserving the high-order fixed point and hence BDF2/space accuracy when `rho(I-omega A_L^{-1}A_H)<1`. Unlike PPE, the admissible low problem is not a constant-density Poisson solve; it is a low-order viscous Helmholtz operator sharing `mu`, `rho`, boundary topology, and periodic quotient constraints, while cross-derivative/high CCD terms remain in the high residual. Implementation: added factor-reused low-order viscous Helmholtz DC solver; implicit-BDF2 viscosity now selects `defect_correction` or `gmres` explicitly via `viscosity.solver.kind` in YAML, with DC as default and GMRES retained only as explicit comparison/diagnostic path; propagated solver tolerances/restart/DC correction controls through RunCfg/runtime/build contexts; canonical ch14/ch13 YAMLs now declare the DC solver. Validation: local `py_compile` PASS; local targeted pytest PASS for GMRES YAML selection, GMRES zero-operator, DC zero-operator, DC residual reduction, and ch14 stack config; remote `make test` PASS for `implicit_bdf2_viscosity_solver_selects_gmres`, `implicit_bdf2_viscous`, `ch14_capillary_yaml_uses_true_low_order_defect_base`, and `test_construction_uniform`; `git diff --check` PASS; paper `latexmk -xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode -halt-on-error main.tex` PASS (`paper/main.pdf`, 241 pp; existing underfull/float diagnostics only). Source commits: `39b4e386`, `0d89f976`, `9ef67ee4`. Main merge not performed. [SOLID-X] new responsibility isolated in `simulation.viscous_helmholtz_dc`; predictor selects injected solver mode rather than fallback branching; config parsing, runtime state, and numerical solve remain separated; no tested code deleted. |

Validation of hypotheses: disabling reinit preserved seam but blew up at `t=7.784`, rejecting ‚Äúreinit is merely harmful‚Äù; isolated RidgeEikonal on a perfectly half-period-symmetric mode-2 input produced `psi_half=3.27e-02` in one call, identifying reinit as symmetry-breaking operator; decomposed diagnostics showed FMM without ridge seeds preserved half symmetry, while ridge mask generated non-equivariant artificial zero seeds; wall-contact mass-correction also treated periodic x-image planes as walls. Fix: default RidgeEikonal no longer promotes Gaussian ridge-mask nodes to zero Dirichlet seeds; optional legacy `ridge_zero_seeds` flag retained off by default; wall-contact protected band is restricted to actual wall axes. Tests: local `test_ridge_eikonal_preserves_mixed_periodic_half_period_symmetry` and wall-contact regression PASS; remote same test PASS; existing mixed periodic velocity/FCCD scalar tests PASS. Paper: formalized periodic quotient space, reinitializer closure/equivariance, true-zero-set-only Eikonal seed rule, wall-axis-only contact pinning, and periodic cyclic face-to-node reconstruction in `paper/sections/05_reinitialization.tex`, `03d_ridge_eikonal.tex`, `10d_ridge_eikonal_nonuniform.tex`, and `appendix_numerics_solver_s4.tex`. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-GRID-IFACE-004 DONE 2026-05-01 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„Äå„Éñ„É≠„Éº„Ç¢„ÉÉ„Éó„Åì„Åù„Åó„Å¶„ÅÑ„Å™„ÅÑ„Åå„ÄÅË®àÁÆó„Å®„Åó„Å¶„ÅØÁÝ¥Á∂ª„Åó„Å¶„ÅÑ„Çã„ÄÇ„Åæ„ÅöÂ∑¶Âè≥ÂØæÁß∞„Å´„Å™„Å£„Å¶„ÅÑ„Å™„ÅÑ„ÅÆ„Åå„Åä„Åã„Åó„ÅÑ„ÄÇÂ¢ÉÁïå„Åß„ÅÆÊºîÁÆó„ÅåÈñìÈÅï„Å£„Å¶„ÅÑ„Å™„ÅÑ„ÅãÔºü„Äç„Åä„Çà„Å≥„ÄåÁêÜË´ñ„Å´Âü∫„Å•„Åç„Åó„Å£„Åã„Çä„Å®ÂÆüË£Ö„Åó„Å¶„ÄÇÂÆüË£ÖÂæå„ÅÆÁ¢∫Ë™ç„ÇíÂøò„Çå„Å™„ÅÑ„Çà„ÅÜ„Å´„ÄÇ„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇTheory: x-periodic/y-wall capillary wave lives in the periodic quotient space; nodal image planes on periodic axes are not independent DOFs for pressure, velocity, or transported scalars, and face-to-node reconstruction on a periodic normal axis must use cyclic adjacent faces rather than one-sided endpoint copying. Root cause: mixed-boundary velocity BC only zeroed wall axes and left periodic image nodes unsynchronised; face-native projection reconstructed periodic nodes with wall-like one-sided endpoints; FCCD/DCCD scalar advection and reinitialization paths could return unsynchronised periodic image planes. Fix: added shared periodic-image synchronization in boundary core; applied it to velocity BCs, FCCD/FVM face reconstruction, FCCD/DCCD advection, and all reinitializer strategies; periodic face reconstruction now uses cyclic face averages and synchronizes passive periodic axes. Validation: local py_compile PASS; local targeted pytest PASS (`mixed_periodic_wall`, `periodic_image`, `fccd_advection_syncs_mixed_periodic_scalar_image`); remote targeted pytest PASS (`mixed_periodic_wall`, `periodic_image`, `fccd_advection_syncs_mixed_periodic_scalar_image`); remote `make cycle EXP=experiment/run.py ARGS="--config _tmp_ch14_capillary_n32_t1"` PASS to `T=1.0`; symmetry diagnostics from snapshots show `psi_gap=p_gap=u_gap=v_gap=0.0` for all saved times, `psi_half_max=2.285e-07`, `v_half_max=9.374e-08`, reflection residuals O(1e-16), `KE_last=3.701584e-05`. Note: two remote pytest attempts failed only due Make/pytest argument quoting/path mistakes before rerun with correct `twophase/...` paths. [SOLID-X] periodic quotient operation is centralized in `core.boundary`; no tested production implementation deleted. |

Design: `grid.domain.boundary` is the single boundary declaration (`wall` or `periodic`); `grid.distribution.axes.<axis>.type` is now only `uniform|nonuniform`; nonuniform axes must declare `monitors.interface` and/or `monitors.wall`. Wall monitors are axis-local, require `grid.domain.boundary: wall`, and use `apply_to` for lower/upper side filtering; periodic domains allow interface monitors but reject wall monitors instead of silently dropping them. Legacy compact interface-only `type/method/alpha` remains accepted, but it cannot express wall refinement; old `grid.distribution.wall`, top-level axis-map `type/method/alpha`, fake `interface_fitted alpha:1.0`, and uniform axes with monitors are rejected. Implementation: refactored YAML parser in `src/twophase/simulation/config_sections.py`; updated canonical ch14 YAMLs, ch13 config builders, config README, and regression tests. Canonical capillary YAML now has x=`nonuniform` with wall monitor only and y=`nonuniform` with interface monitor only, yielding `fitting_axes=(False, True)` and `wall_refinement_axes=(True, False)`. Evidence: observed signed m=2 acceleration is restoring; `bf_residual_max` crosses `1e2` at step 2061 before divergence and KE failure; `kappa_max` is capped from the first step; fitted nonuniform grid with alpha=2 produces wall-contact nodal curvature spikes (`321.5` psi-direct filtered, `340.2` legacy filtered) while excluding two boundary layers gives physical O(1.5) curvature and uniform alpha=1 remains O(1.58). Therefore bulk curvature extrapolated to a pinned no-slip side-wall contact lacks the wall reaction/contact-angle/contact-root geometry closure required for a discrete Young-Laplace energy gradient; the affine jump then maps the spurious nodal jump to cut-face sources. Recorded design direction: introduce a shared capillary geometry state for interface/cut-face quadrature, wall-compatible contact closure, and energy-work audit before changing production behavior. Paper reflection: `paper/sections/03b_cls_transport.tex` now states the no-slip wall phase-topology invariant (`sign(psi_w-1/2)` and fixed contact root set, without freezing same-phase wall trace values; periodic boundaries excluded); `paper/sections/11_full_algorithm.tex` now warns that wall-contact curvature must be treated as interface/cut-face geometry rather than raw bulk interface-band curvature. Artifact: `artifacts/A/ch14_capillary_energy_wall_curvature_rca_CHK-RA-GPU-UTIL-013.md`. | session_CHK | CHK-RA-GPU-UTIL-006 DONE 2026-05-01 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåFDÁõ¥Êé•Ê≥ï„Å®CGÊ≥ï„ÅßÁ≤æÂ∫¶„Éª‰ΩøÁî®„É°„É¢„É™„ÉªË®àÁÆóÊôÇÈñì„ÅÆÈñ¢‰øÇ„ÇíË©ï‰æ°„Åó„Å¶„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇSame retained worktree `.claude/worktrees/ra-gpu-util-20260501` / branch `ra-gpu-util-20260501` continued. Scope: add reproducible benchmark `experiment/ch14/bench_fd_ll_solvers.py` and evaluate FD direct vs weighted-CG for the DC low-order `L_L` solve. Method: remote GPU, conservative FD flux-form `L_L`, wall Neumann + gauge pin, 3 RHS per fixed density field to mimic one DC solve; direct path factors once and reuses factor; CG solves weighted SPD `-W L_L p=-W rhs` with Jacobi for tol `{1e-4,1e-6,1e-8}` and N `{64,128,192}`. Result: direct residual `~1e-14`; CG residual tracks tolerance, with relative error vs direct `O(1e-4..1e-3)` at tol `1e-4`, `O(1e-6)` at tol `1e-6`, `O(1e-8)` at tol `1e-8`; direct is faster by `2.1x--9.4x` for current 2D/RHS=3 workload; CG uses only `7.5%--10%` of direct CuPy pool memory. Decision: keep `fd/direct` default for existing ch14 capillary/schedule=1 runs; use FD-CG only for memory pressure or larger/3D cases, and set tol around outer DC tolerance (`~1e-8`) for paper-grade accuracy. Validation: `make cycle EXP=experiment/ch14/bench_fd_ll_solvers.py ARGS="--sizes 64,128,192 --cg-tols 1e-4,1e-6,1e-8 --rhs-count 3 --maxiter 3000 --output experiment/ch14/results/fd_ll_solver_bench/fd_ll_solver_bench.json"` PASS; smoke run PASS; `git diff --check` PASS. Artifact: `artifacts/A/ch14_fd_direct_vs_cg_bench_CHK-RA-GPU-UTIL-006.md`. [SOLID-X] benchmark/artifact only; production solver boundaries unchanged. |

| session_CHK | CHK-RA-GPU-UTIL-004 DONE 2026-05-01 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåDC„Å´„Åä„ÅÑ„Å¶„ÄÅFD direct„Å´Êõø„Åà„Å¶CG„Å™„Å©„ÇÇ‰Ωø„Åà„Çã„Çà„ÅÜ„Å´„ÅØ„Åó„Å¶„Åä„Åç„Åü„ÅÑ„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇSame retained worktree `.claude/worktrees/ra-gpu-util-20260501` / branch `ra-gpu-util-20260501` continued after prior main no-ff merge. Scope: keep production ch14 `fd/direct` base as measured fast path, but add optional `fd_iterative` low-order `L_L` correction solver for DC. Theory/design: CG is allowed only on the control-volume-weighted SPD equivalent `-W L_L p = -W rhs` because the row-scaled FD/FVM operator is not Euclidean-symmetric at boundary/control-volume rows; `line_pcr` is rejected for CG as nonsymmetric; `fd_iterative` disables direct fallback so the configured iterative path cannot silently become direct. Implementation: added `PPESolverFDMatrixFree`, backend CG helper, parser/factory/runtime registration and aliases (`fd_iterative`, `fd_matrixfree`, `fd_cg`), docs and tests. Validation: remote `make test PYTEST_ARGS='-k fd_iterative -q'` PASS (2); `make test PYTEST_ARGS='-k fd_matrixfree -q'` PASS (1, CG matches `PPESolverFDDirect`); `make test PYTEST_ARGS='-k fvm_matrixfree -q'` PASS (8); `make test PYTEST_ARGS='-k defect_correction -q'` PASS (11); `git diff --check` PASS. Artifact: `artifacts/A/ch14_fd_iterative_cg_option_CHK-RA-GPU-UTIL-004.md`. [SOLID-X] no SOLID violation found; additive `IPPESolver` implementation and existing registry/runtime seams used. |

Design/implementation: clear pressure-jump context for neutral velocity reprojection PPE solves, and precompute wall CCD derivative matrices `D1/D2` from the same compact wall system for GPU no-override paths, preserving metric mapping `J dŒæ` and `J¬≤ dŒæŒæ + J dJ dŒæ`. Verification: remote `make test PYTEST_ARGS='-k reprojector_ -q'` PASS (2), `-k ccd_derivative_gpu_matches_cpu --gpu -q` PASS (1), `-k fccd_face_gradient_gpu_matches_cpu --gpu -q` PASS (1). Target 80% not reached under N=128/schedule=1; remaining blocker is GMRES/FCCD small-kernel granularity and mandatory per-step grid rebuild/reprojection, not schedule relaxation.
