| session_CHK | CHK-RA-CAPWAVE-N32T8-RCA-001 DONE 2026-04-30 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„ÄåN=32,T=8 ÊØõÁÆ°Ê≥¢„ÅßÊúüÂæÖ„Åô„ÇãÊåØÂπÖÊ∏õË°∞„ÅåË¶ã„Çâ„Çå„Å™„ÅÑÂéüÂõÝ„ÇíÁâ©ÁêÜ„ÉªÊï∞Â≠¶ÁêÜË´ñ„Å´Âü∫„Å•„ÅçÂ§öÊï∞‰ªÆË™¨Âåñ„ÅóÊ§úË®º„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇRemote-first controls: baseline `NU_r20` (nonuniform reinit20, T=8), `NU_noR` (nonuniform reinit effectively off, T=3.5), `U_r20` (uniform reinit20, T=3.5), `NU_r46` (nonuniform reinit46, T=3.5; SSH reset after completion, results pulled by `make pull`). Finding: stored `interface_amplitude` is max deviation; signed Fourier `A2` decays/crosses near theory quarter period (`T_q‚âà4.158`, observed near `4.5`), so not a simple Young--Laplace sign error. Failure is high-wavenumber wrinkle/energy creation: baseline interface length `1.003902‚Üí1.454146`, estimated `œÉ(|Œì|-1)+KE` `2.809e-4‚Üí3.493e-2`, high-mode amplitude grows to `2.277e-2`, `kappa_max` cap reached at `t=1.423`. Controls reject pure reinit removal (div/RHS much worse), reject reinit physical-frequency tuning as cure, and implicate nonuniform wall/FCCD affine-jump coupling (`U_r20` much healthier: length `1.005341`, max div `5.45e-3`, max PPE RHS `6.39e-1`). Most likely cause: current affine jump is algebraically balanced for static manufactured jumps but not a discrete variational capillary operator; `J_f` is built from nodal/band curvature averages rather than cut-face/interface quadrature and lacks a nonuniform adjoint corrector energy identity. Memo: `docs/memo/CHK-RA-CAPWAVE-N32T8-RCA-001.md`. [SOLID-X] investigation/docs only; no production code change. |

Main diagnostics: `KE 1.3827e-09 -> 2.6867e-03` (max `2.6903e-03`), `interface_amplitude 1.1970e-02 -> 7.8471e-02` (max `7.9242e-02`), volume drift final `6.3666e-04` / max `6.7475e-04`, `div_u_max` max `4.8288e-02`, `ppe_rhs_max` max `1.3071e+01`, `bf_residual_max` max `3.1796e+01`, `kappa_max=5.0` cap hit `1536/1792` steps. Route diagnostics: affine jump active all steps (`ppe_interface_coupling_affine_jump=1`, legacy jump `0`), global gauge path (`ppe_phase_count=1`, `ppe_pin_count=1`, `ppe_mean_gauge=0`), capillary limiter all steps. Expected trend check: signed m=2 mode acceleration from saved œà snapshots is restoring and near theory: first 6 snapshots `A''=-1.4396e-03` vs theory `-1.4271e-03` (`1.009√ó`), first 8 snapshots `-1.4098e-03` (`0.988√ó`). Interpretation: old wrong-sign/anti-restoring failure is not present and T=10 does not blow up; however long-time amplitude growth and curvature-cap saturation remain geometry/curvature/energy-stability bottlenecks. Memo: `docs/memo/CHK-RA-CH14-N32T10-RERUN-001_capillary_trend.md`. [SOLID-X] experiment execution + memo only; no production code change. |

| session_CHK | CHK-RA-CH9-AFFINE-GAUGE-001 DONE 2026-04-30 ‚Äî User ÊåáÁ§∫„Äåaffine„ÅÆÊÝ∏ÂøÉÊù°‰ª∂„ÄéÊó¢Áü•„Ç∏„É£„É≥„Éó„ÇíÊ∫Ä„Åü„ÅôÂúßÂäõ„Å™„Çâ cut-face flux „ÅåÊ∂à„Åà„Çã„Äè„ÅåÂ£ä„Çå„ÇãÁêÜÁî±„ÇíÁêÜË´ñÁöÑ„Å´Ë£è‰ªò„Åë„Åó„Å¶„ÄÇ„Åù„Çå„Çí„ÇÇ„Å£„Å¶„ÄÅË´ñÊñá„Åã„ÇâÂâäÈô§„Åó„Åü„ÅÑ„ÄçÂØæÂøú„ÄÇTheory: affine cut-face flux is one coupled face operator `F_{\Gamma,f}=\alpha_f(G_f(p)-B_f(j_\Gamma))`; the known-jump zero-flux invariant requires the same nonzero cut-face coefficient to multiply both the pressure-gradient part and the jump lifting. A detached phase-block / per-phase Neumann gauge sets the cross-phase pressure coefficient to zero; if the known-jump coefficient remains nonzero, a pressure satisfying the jump still leaves residual cut-face flux, while setting it to zero removes the capillary drive itself. Therefore production affine has one global null mode and uses a fixed global gauge/pin, not phase-mean gauge. Paper: `paper/sections/09b_split_ppe.tex`, `paper/sections/09e_ppe_bc.tex`, `paper/sections/09f_pressure_summary.tex` updated to remove production endorsement of phase-mean gauge and document the invariant failure. Validation: `git diff --check` PASS; `latexmk -xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode -halt-on-error main.tex` PASS (`paper/main.pdf`, 234 pp). [SOLID-X] paper-only; no production code change. |

Memo: `docs/memo/CHK-RA-CH14-N32T1-001_ch93_verification.md`. [SOLID-X] no production code change. |

| CHK-RA-CH14-006 | 2026-04-30 | theory+design | [DONE] Generic interface-stress closure design. Replaces regular-pressure `J=œÉ Œ∫(1-œà)` with jump-aware affine projection `G_Œì(p;j)=G(p)-B_Œì j`, shared by capillary wave, rising bubble, and RT. Defines physical laws P1--P5 and forbidden fixes. |
| CHK-RA-CH14-005 | 2026-04-30 | analysis+rootcause | [DONE] ch14 capillary failure root cause. 40 hypotheses evaluated; primary cause identified as pressure-jump algebraic cancellation (`J` represented as regular pressure field, `L(J)` subtracted, `p_base‚âà-J`, `p_total‚âà0`). Probe ratio `9.02e-4`; CFL/viscosity/walls rejected. |

| CHK-174 | 2026-04-21 | config+diag | [DONE] Activated per-step interface-following grid rebuild for ch13_05 rising bubble dynamic variants. Updated dynamic configs to `grid_rebuild_freq: 1` and removed the [ns_pipeline.py](../src/twophase/simulation/ns_pipeline.py) fallback that silently converted non-uniform `freq=1` to `10`. Local targeted tests 16 passed. Remote GPU confirmed `[dynamic/1 non-uniform] grid built from IC`; dynamic debug BLOWUP step 4 `t=0.0013`, earlier than dynamic/10 step 19. Conclusion: requested every-step following is active, but moving-grid remap/reprojection strongly amplifies the known projection closure issue. Report: [docs/memo/ch13_05_per_step_grid_rebuild.md](memo/ch13_05_per_step_grid_rebuild.md). Branch: worktree-rising-bubble |
| CHK-173 | 2026-04-21 | config+diag | [DONE] Activated dynamic grid fitting variants for ch13_05 rising bubble. Added dynamic configs with `grid_rebuild_freq: 10`; fixed [ns_pipeline.py](../src/twophase/simulation/ns_pipeline.py) so alpha>1 cases always fit the IC grid before time stepping and dynamic rebuilds start after step 0. Local targeted tests 16 passed. Remote GPU confirmed activation via `[dynamic/10 non-uniform] grid built from IC`; dynamic debug BLOWUP step 19 `t=0.004829`, earlier than static step 27 `t=0.007149`. Conclusion: logic is active, but moving-grid remap/reprojection worsens the already-known projection closure issue. Report: [docs/memo/ch13_05_dynamic_grid_activation.md](memo/ch13_05_dynamic_grid_activation.md). Branch: worktree-rising-bubble |
| CHK-172 | 2026-04-21 | poc+diag | [DONE] Face-flux projection opt-in PoC. Added `run.face_flux_projection` default-off flag + `ch13_05_rising_bubble_fullstack_alpha2_faceproj_debug.yaml`. Implemented face flux / harmonic PPE pressure flux / opt-in projection helpers in [gradient_operator.py](../src/twophase/simulation/gradient_operator.py), gated in [ns_pipeline.py](../src/twophase/simulation/ns_pipeline.py). Targeted local `test_ns_pipeline_fccd.py` 6 passed. Remote GPU: default FVM-D path BLOWUP step 27 `t=0.007149`; faceproj opt-in BLOWUP step 29 `t=0.007532`; strict face-preserving reconstruction worsened to step 23 and was restored. Conclusion: partial timing improvement only; nodal reconstruction/wall BC breaks exact projected face flux. Next production path must carry face/staggered flux as projection state. Report: [docs/memo/ch13_05_face_projection_poc.md](memo/ch13_05_face_projection_poc.md). Branch: worktree-rising-bubble |
| CHK-171 | 2026-04-21 | theory+diag+partial-fix | [DONE] ch13_05 rising-bubble alpha=2 root cause hypothesis/controls. Added controls `sigma0`, `g0`, `noforce`, `alpha1`; remote GPU showed alpha=2 baseline / sigma0 / g0 all BLOWUP near `t‚âà0.007`, no-force alpha=2 exactly static to `T=0.02`, alpha=1 stable to `T=0.02`. Identified non-uniform-grid projection closure mismatch: FVM PPE solves face harmonic `rho_face` operator, corrector applies nodal `rho_i^{-1}` averaged gradient, so `D_FVM M^{-1}G != A_FVM`. Added partial PoC [gradient_operator.py](../src/twophase/simulation/gradient_operator.py) `FVMDivergenceOperator` / `CCDDivergenceOperator` and [ns_pipeline.py](../src/twophase/simulation/ns_pipeline.py) paired divergence for PPE RHS + debug divergence; targeted local `test_ns_pipeline_fccd.py` 6 passed. Remote GPU PoC reduced max residual but still BLOWUP, confirming RHS-only fix is insufficient. Production remedy: face-flux projection/corrector + conservative face-to-node reconstruction. Report: [docs/memo/ch13_05_rising_bubble_root_cause_hypotheses.md](memo/ch13_05_rising_bubble_root_cause_hypotheses.md). Branch: worktree-rising-bubble |
| CHK-170 | 2026-04-21 | exp+diag | [DONE] ResearchArchitect rising-bubble probe in new worktree. Added [ch13_05_rising_bubble_fullstack_alpha2.yaml](../experiment/ch13/config/ch13_05_rising_bubble_fullstack_alpha2.yaml), based on `ch13_04_capwave_fullstack_alpha2.yaml` numerical stack (FCCD flux advection/convection, `ridge_eikonal`, `alpha_grid=2.0`, `consistent_gfm`) with bubble deltas (`LX=1, LY=2, NX=64, NY=128`, gas circle `R=0.25`, `g_acc=0.001`). Fixed remote-GPU blocker in [velocity_reprojector.py](../src/twophase/simulation/velocity_reprojector.py) by returning PPE/CCD correction arrays to `backend.to_device` before mixed backend arithmetic. Remote GPU primary run wrote [data.npz](../experiment/ch13/results/ch13_05_rising_bubble_fullstack_alpha2/data.npz) but hit **BLOWUP step 27, t=0.007014** with volume drift `2.4e-16` and KE `1.82e6`. Added [ch13_05_rising_bubble_fullstack_alpha2_debug.yaml](../experiment/ch13/config/ch13_05_rising_bubble_fullstack_alpha2_debug.yaml); debug remote GPU run showed `bf_residual_max: 4.4e2 -> 1.5e12`, `ppe_rhs_max=3.87e11`, `div_u_max=1.60e5`, identifying momentum/pressure balanced-force residual rather than CLS mass loss. Comparison remote GPU run of `ch13_02_waterair_bubble` reached t=1.277 before KE threshold. Full report: [docs/memo/ch13_05_rising_bubble_fullstack_alpha2_report.md](memo/ch13_05_rising_bubble_fullstack_alpha2_report.md). Commits: `bb97aee`, `3826d3f`, `41eef12`, `<pending>`. Branch: worktree-rising-bubble |

# ¬ß LESSONS (KL-01..12)

## ¬ßA ‚Äî Known Error Classes (Math/Code)

| KL | Failure | Fix Pattern |
|---|---|---|
| KL-01 | Block matrix (2,1) sign flip after RHS transposition | Read RHS coeff ‚Üí negate ‚Üí write to LHS |
| KL-02 | Wrong block size (3√ó3 vs 2√ó2) in docs | Verify block dims against actual code arrays |
| KL-03 | Pseudocode comment names wrong algorithm | Cross-check comment vs accumulation pattern |
| KL-04 | D(Œ∫f) ‚âÝ Œ∫D(f) for varying Œ∫ | Expand D(Œ∫f); never factor variable coefficients |
| KL-05 | Nyquist modified wavenumber ‚âÝ finite-grid spectral radius | Compute spectral radius of actual discrete matrix |
| KL-06 | Pre-asymptotic O(h‚Å¥) mistaken for asymptotic | Confirm slope stability over ‚â•3 grid doublings |
| KL-07 | "Conservative" CFL rounding wrong direction | Smaller dt: floor dt, ceiling Nsteps |
| KL-08 | Kronecker C-order vs Fortran-order confusion | State convention explicitly; verify with N=2 example |
| KL-09 | PPE LGMRES-primary/LU-fallback vs LU-primary confusion | LGMRES primary; spsolve auto-fallback on non-convergence (pre-2026-04-15) |
| KL-10 | Collocated corrector "exact CCD-div-free" claim | RC PPE gives ‚Äñ‚àá_RC¬∑u^{n+1}‚Äñ=0; CCD residual remains O(h¬≤) |
| KL-11 | Pin-node excl still targets (0,0) after move to center | Use `pin_dof = ravel_multi_index(tuple(n//2 for n in grid.N), grid.shape)` |
| KL-12 | `\texorpdfstring` missing in math heading ‚Üí xelatex infinite loop | Wrap numbered math headings; pre-compile grep required |
